COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Emma
Teacher: Huryv Osmanova
Level: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday 2025/2026
Absences: 0

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent

Emma is a polite and well-mannered student who generally shows a positive attitude in class. She is calm, respectful, and attentive, and she works well with both her teacher and classmates. At times, she can be a little hesitant to speak up, but with encouragement she is willing to try and becomes more confident. She responds well when given time to think and can be very receptive to guidance. Her kind and cooperative nature contributes positively to the classroom atmosphere.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good

Emma takes part in class activities and usually completes the work she is asked to do with care. She has shown good effort throughout the term, especially when speaking and working with familiar structures. She can make sentences using the target language and is able to follow the lesson content well. However, she sometimes needs encouragement to try more independently and to avoid relying too much on French. Continuing to build confidence and expanding her vocabulary will help her participate more freely and consistently.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

Emma has made good progress this term. She understands the new vocabulary well and can recognise and use many of the structures covered in class. She is also able to read the stories and chapters well, and her reading is often impressive. In oral activities, she can answer using full structures and usually corrects herself when she makes mistakes, which is very positive. She still needs to work on pronunciation, connectors, and some grammar points such as the third person singular, pronouns, and tense accuracy. With regular practice and more confidence, she will continue to improve.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 7.10 Good.

Emma performs well orally when she feels comfortable and has enough time to think. She can repeat and use the target language accurately in many situations, and her pronunciation is generally improving. However, she still makes occasional pronunciation mistakes and sometimes needs support to expand her answers.

Writing Mark: 6.80 Good.

Emma's written work shows a good understanding of the language covered this term. She can complete grammar and vocabulary tasks quite well. However, she still makes some errors with capital letters, punctuation, and grammar when writing independently. Further practice with writing and careful revision before tests will help her gain more confidence and accuracy.

Homework mark: 6.50 Acceptable.

Emma has completed some homework and has generally shown a reasonable effort. To improve further, she should make a more consistent effort to practise at home, revise vocabulary regularly, and review the grammar points covered in class.

Final comment

Over the course of the term, Emma has shown a positive attitude and has made steady progress in class. She is polite, attentive, and increasingly willing to try, especially when she is given a bit of time and support. Her reading skills are particularly strong, and she shows good understanding of the content covered in class. To continue improving, she should focus on speaking more confidently, using English as much as possible, and revising vocabulary and grammar regularly. With continued encouragement and practice, Emma is well placed to make further progress next term.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Behaviour, Work, Performance) plus Marks and a Final comment, as shown in the examples. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. All observations must be grounded in the source notes. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections (Behaviour/Work/Performance) + Marks + Final comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - tone: constructive and honest, matches examples - balance: weaknesses (pronunciation errors, reliance on French, avoiding speaking) acknowledged alongside strengths "}]

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Emma
Teacher: Huryv Osmanova
Level: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday 2025/2026
Absences: 0

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent

Emma is a polite and well-mannered student who generally shows a positive attitude in class. She is calm, respectful, and attentive, and she works well with both her teacher and classmates. At times, she can be a little hesitant to speak up, but with encouragement she is willing to try and becomes more confident. She responds well when given time to think and can be very receptive to guidance. Her kind and cooperative nature contributes positively to the classroom atmosphere.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good

Emma takes part in class activities and usually completes the work she is asked to do with care. She has shown good effort throughout the term, especially when speaking and working with familiar structures. She can make sentences using the target language and is able to follow the lesson content well. However, she sometimes needs encouragement to try more independently and to avoid relying too much on French. Continuing to build confidence and expanding her vocabulary will help her participate more freely and consistently.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

Emma has made good progress this term. She understands the new vocabulary well and can recognise and use many of the structures covered in class. She is also able to read the stories and chapters well, and her reading is often impressive. In oral activities, she can answer using full structures and usually corrects herself when she makes mistakes, which is very positive. She still needs to work on pronunciation, connectors, and some grammar points such as the third person singular, pronouns, and tense accuracy. With regular practice and more confidence, she will continue to improve.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 7.10 Good.

Emma performs well orally when she feels comfortable and has enough time to think. She can repeat and use the target language accurately in many situations, and her pronunciation is generally improving. However, she still makes occasional pronunciation mistakes and sometimes needs support to expand her answers.

Writing Mark: 6.80 Good.

Emma's written work shows a good understanding of the language covered this term. She can complete grammar and vocabulary tasks quite well. However, she still makes some errors with capital letters, punctuation, and grammar when writing independently. Further practice with writing and careful revision before tests will help her gain more confidence and accuracy.

Homework mark: 6.50 Acceptable.

Emma has completed some homework and has generally shown a reasonable effort. To improve further, she should make a more consistent effort to practise at home, revise vocabulary regularly, and review the grammar points covered in class.

Final comment

Over the course of the term, Emma has shown a positive attitude and has made steady progress in class. She is polite, attentive, and increasingly willing to try, especially when she is given a bit of time and support. Her reading skills are particularly strong, and she shows good understanding of the content covered in class. To continue improving, she should focus on speaking more confidently, using English as much as possible, and revising vocabulary and grammar regularly. With continued encouragement and practice, Emma is well placed to make further progress next term.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Behaviour, Work, Performance) plus Marks and a Final comment, as shown in the examples. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. All observations must be grounded in the source notes. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections (Behaviour/Work/Performance) + Marks + Final comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - tone: constructive and honest, matches examples - balance: weaknesses (pronunciation errors, reliance on French, avoiding speaking) acknowledged alongside strengths

response.output:
COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Emma
Teacher: Huruv Osmanova
Level: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday
Absences: 0

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

  • Emma maintains a polite and attentive demeanor in class. She is respectful toward her peers and teacher, and she asks for help when needed.
  • She occasionally asks for vocabulary words in French when she lacks the English equivalents, though she does attempt to use English when prompted.
  • Emma can be defensive about writing tasks but participates willingly when given oral exercises or collaborative activities.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good

  • Emma engages actively in class discussions and activities, particularly when given time to process answers. She demonstrates improved confidence in oral tasks and is willing to make efforts to get the right answers.
  • She performs well in reading tasks, with impressive pronunciation in some instances, though she occasionally makes errors with word endings or third-person singular forms.
  • Emma shows responsibility in her learning by reviewing grammatical structures and using target language when prompted, though she sometimes needs reminders about specific rules (e.g., the -ing form after "used to").
  • She participates in collaborative work but may avoid individual tasks, sometimes requesting to leave the room temporarily.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

  • Emma demonstrates a solid understanding of vocabulary and grammatical structures, particularly in listening and reading activities. She can summarize texts, form questions, and answer using target language with support.
  • She struggles at times with pronunciation, especially with certain words, but is able to correct herself when prompted.
  • Emma shows improvement in using connectors (e.g., "although," "despite") and structures like "would rather… than…" and "prefer… to…," though she occasionally forgets tense markers or subject-verb agreement.
  • Her writing contains errors in capitalization and punctuation, and she sometimes lacks confidence in expressing herself fully in written tasks.

Final comment

Emma has shown steady progress this term, particularly in oral participation and reading fluency. She is attentive and cooperative, though she sometimes hesitates to take risks in speaking or writing. With continued encouragement to use English consistently and practice pronunciation, she will further develop her confidence and accuracy. Her willingness to engage in activities and seek help when needed reflects a positive attitude toward learning.

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, Tuesday & Friday

---

Term report

Data

  • Name: Emma
  • Class: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday

Behaviour

Comment: Emma is a polite and attentive student who is usually motivated and interested in class. She is more comfortable asking questions when she has time to think, and she becomes more confident when she is given more time or when answers are not given straight away. At times, she can seem a little uneasy or defensive when she does not understand something, but she is willing to make more effort and try when encouraged.

Work

Comment: Emma takes part well in activities and works seriously. She uses the target language well in gapped sentences and can use grammatical structures from previous chapters. She participates well in collaborative work and is more willing to speak when supported, although she still sometimes tries to avoid speaking and needs encouragement to expand her answers. She also sometimes asks for vocabulary in French and needs help with pronunciation, vocabulary, and writing tasks.

Performance

Comment: Emma reads very well, and her reading was particularly impressive. She can summarise chapters, answer questions, use the target language from the board, and manage listening activities well. She has also shown that she can use structures such as the causative form, comparatives, would rather...than..., prefer...to..., and the language needed to create problems and solutions. She still makes some pronunciation, pronoun, tense, third-person singular -s, spelling, capital letter, and punctuation mistakes, and she is not always confident when giving full answers or using new vocabulary in original sentences.

Final comment

Emma is a polite and attentive student who has become more confident when given time and support. She can use several target language structures well and reads impressively. With continued encouragement to try more often, speak a little more freely, and keep working on pronunciation, grammar, and vocabulary, she will continue to improve.

Kids&Us.

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Emma
Teacher: Huryv Osmanova
Level: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday 2025/2026
Absences: 0

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent

Emma is a polite and well-mannered student who generally shows a positive attitude in class. She is calm, respectful, and attentive, and she works well with both her teacher and classmates. At times, she can be a little hesitant to speak up, but with encouragement she is willing to try and becomes more confident. She responds well when given time to think and can be very receptive to guidance. Her kind and cooperative nature contributes positively to the classroom atmosphere.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good

Emma takes part in class activities and usually completes the work she is asked to do with care. She has shown good effort throughout the term, especially when speaking and working with familiar structures. She can make sentences using the target language and is able to follow the lesson content well. However, she sometimes needs encouragement to try more independently and to avoid relying too much on French. Continuing to build confidence and expanding her vocabulary will help her participate more freely and consistently.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

Emma has made good progress this term. She understands the new vocabulary well and can recognise and use many of the structures covered in class. She is also able to read the stories and chapters well, and her reading is often impressive. In oral activities, she can answer using full structures and usually corrects herself when she makes mistakes, which is very positive. She still needs to work on pronunciation, connectors, and some grammar points such as the third person singular, pronouns, and tense accuracy. With regular practice and more confidence, she will continue to improve.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 7.10 Good.

Emma performs well orally when she feels comfortable and has enough time to think. She can repeat and use the target language accurately in many situations, and her pronunciation is generally improving. However, she still makes occasional pronunciation mistakes and sometimes needs support to expand her answers.

Writing Mark: 6.80 Good.

Emma's written work shows a good understanding of the language covered this term. She can complete grammar and vocabulary tasks quite well. However, she still makes some errors with capital letters, punctuation, and grammar when writing independently. Further practice with writing and careful revision before tests will help her gain more confidence and accuracy.

Homework mark: 6.50 Acceptable.

Emma has completed some homework and has generally shown a reasonable effort. To improve further, she should make a more consistent effort to practise at home, revise vocabulary regularly, and review the grammar points covered in class.

Final comment

Over the course of the term, Emma has shown a positive attitude and has made steady progress in class. She is polite, attentive, and increasingly willing to try, especially when she is given a bit of time and support. Her reading skills are particularly strong, and she shows good understanding of the content covered in class. To continue improving, she should focus on speaking more confidently, using English as much as possible, and revising vocabulary and grammar regularly. With continued encouragement and practice, Emma is well placed to make further progress next term.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Behaviour, Work, Performance) plus Marks and a Final comment, as shown in the examples. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. All observations must be grounded in the source notes. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections (Behaviour/Work/Performance) + Marks + Final comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - tone: constructive and honest, matches examples - balance: weaknesses (pronunciation errors, reliance on French, avoiding speaking) acknowledged alongside strengths "}]

response.output:
Kids&Us.

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, Tuesday & Friday

---

Term report

Data

  • Name: Emma
  • Class: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday

Behaviour

Comment: Emma is a polite and attentive student who is usually motivated and interested in class. She is more comfortable asking questions when she has time to think, and she becomes more confident when she is given more time or when answers are not given straight away. At times, she can seem a little uneasy or defensive when she does not understand something, but she is willing to make more effort and try when encouraged.

Work

Comment: Emma takes part well in activities and works seriously. She uses the target language well in gapped sentences and can use grammatical structures from previous chapters. She participates well in collaborative work and is more willing to speak when supported, although she still sometimes tries to avoid speaking and needs encouragement to expand her answers. She also sometimes asks for vocabulary in French and needs help with pronunciation, vocabulary, and writing tasks.

Performance

Comment: Emma reads very well, and her reading was particularly impressive. She can summarise chapters, answer questions, use the target language from the board, and manage listening activities well. She has also shown that she can use structures such as the causative form, comparatives, would rather...than..., prefer...to..., and the language needed to create problems and solutions. She still makes some pronunciation, pronoun, tense, third-person singular -s, spelling, capital letter, and punctuation mistakes, and she is not always confident when giving full answers or using new vocabulary in original sentences.

Final comment

Emma is a polite and attentive student who has become more confident when given time and support. She can use several target language structures well and reads impressively. With continued encouragement to try more often, speak a little more freely, and keep working on pronunciation, grammar, and vocabulary, she will continue to improve.

Behaviour

Work

Assessment (Work): Good
Comment: Emma demonstrates a solid commitment to her studies and participates actively in class activities. She handles grammar vocabulary and target language structures with competence, though she occasionally needs reminders about specific grammatical rules. While she initially avoids speaking, she engages more when encouraged and shows willingness to make efforts. Her pronunciation and occasional tense mistakes are areas for improvement, but she corrects herself when prompted.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good
Comment: Emma performs well in reading tasks and demonstrates a good grasp of vocabulary and grammatical structures. She can summarize chapters, create questions, and answer using the target language effectively. Her ability to understand and participate in listening activities is strong, though pronunciation and fluency require ongoing practice.

Final comment

Emma has shown consistent dedication and a positive attitude in her learning. She actively participates in class discussions and contributes to a collaborative environment. While she excels in reading and understanding vocabulary, she should continue to work on pronunciation, fluency, and speaking confidence. With continued encouragement and practice, she is well-positioned to further develop her language skills.

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Emma
Teacher: Huryv Osmanova
Level: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday 2025/2026
Absences: 0

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent

Emma is a polite and well-mannered student who generally shows a positive attitude in class. She is calm, respectful, and attentive, and she works well with both her teacher and classmates. At times, she can be a little hesitant to speak up, but with encouragement she is willing to try and becomes more confident. She responds well when given time to think and can be very receptive to guidance. Her kind and cooperative nature contributes positively to the classroom atmosphere.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good

Emma takes part in class activities and usually completes the work she is asked to do with care. She has shown good effort throughout the term, especially when speaking and working with familiar structures. She can make sentences using the target language and is able to follow the lesson content well. However, she sometimes needs encouragement to try more independently and to avoid relying too much on French. Continuing to build confidence and expanding her vocabulary will help her participate more freely and consistently.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

Emma has made good progress this term. She understands the new vocabulary well and can recognise and use many of the structures covered in class. She is also able to read the stories and chapters well, and her reading is often impressive. In oral activities, she can answer using full structures and usually corrects herself when she makes mistakes, which is very positive. She still needs to work on pronunciation, connectors, and some grammar points such as the third person singular, pronouns, and tense accuracy. With regular practice and more confidence, she will continue to improve.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 7.10 Good.

Emma performs well orally when she feels comfortable and has enough time to think. She can repeat and use the target language accurately in many situations, and her pronunciation is generally improving. However, she still makes occasional pronunciation mistakes and sometimes needs support to expand her answers.

Writing Mark: 6.80 Good.

Emma's written work shows a good understanding of the language covered this term. She can complete grammar and vocabulary tasks quite well. However, she still makes some errors with capital letters, punctuation, and grammar when writing independently. Further practice with writing and careful revision before tests will help her gain more confidence and accuracy.

Homework mark: 6.50 Acceptable.

Emma has completed some homework and has generally shown a reasonable effort. To improve further, she should make a more consistent effort to practise at home, revise vocabulary regularly, and review the grammar points covered in class.

Final comment

Over the course of the term, Emma has shown a positive attitude and has made steady progress in class. She is polite, attentive, and increasingly willing to try, especially when she is given a bit of time and support. Her reading skills are particularly strong, and she shows good understanding of the content covered in class. To continue improving, she should focus on speaking more confidently, using English as much as possible, and revising vocabulary and grammar regularly. With continued encouragement and practice, Emma is well placed to make further progress next term.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Behaviour, Work, Performance) plus Marks and a Final comment, as shown in the examples. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. All observations must be grounded in the source notes. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections (Behaviour/Work/Performance) + Marks + Final comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - tone: constructive and honest, matches examples - balance: weaknesses (pronunciation errors, reliance on French, avoiding speaking) acknowledged alongside strengths "}]

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Emma
Teacher: Huryv Osmanova
Level: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday 2025/2026
Absences: 0

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent

Emma is a polite and well-mannered student who generally shows a positive attitude in class. She is calm, respectful, and attentive, and she works well with both her teacher and classmates. At times, she can be a little hesitant to speak up, but with encouragement she is willing to try and becomes more confident. She responds well when given time to think and can be very receptive to guidance. Her kind and cooperative nature contributes positively to the classroom atmosphere.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good

Emma takes part in class activities and usually completes the work she is asked to do with care. She has shown good effort throughout the term, especially when speaking and working with familiar structures. She can make sentences using the target language and is able to follow the lesson content well. However, she sometimes needs encouragement to try more independently and to avoid relying too much on French. Continuing to build confidence and expanding her vocabulary will help her participate more freely and consistently.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

Emma has made good progress this term. She understands the new vocabulary well and can recognise and use many of the structures covered in class. She is also able to read the stories and chapters well, and her reading is often impressive. In oral activities, she can answer using full structures and usually corrects herself when she makes mistakes, which is very positive. She still needs to work on pronunciation, connectors, and some grammar points such as the third person singular, pronouns, and tense accuracy. With regular practice and more confidence, she will continue to improve.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 7.10 Good.

Emma performs well orally when she feels comfortable and has enough time to think. She can repeat and use the target language accurately in many situations, and her pronunciation is generally improving. However, she still makes occasional pronunciation mistakes and sometimes needs support to expand her answers.

Writing Mark: 6.80 Good.

Emma's written work shows a good understanding of the language covered this term. She can complete grammar and vocabulary tasks quite well. However, she still makes some errors with capital letters, punctuation, and grammar when writing independently. Further practice with writing and careful revision before tests will help her gain more confidence and accuracy.

Homework mark: 6.50 Acceptable.

Emma has completed some homework and has generally shown a reasonable effort. To improve further, she should make a more consistent effort to practise at home, revise vocabulary regularly, and review the grammar points covered in class.

Final comment

Over the course of the term, Emma has shown a positive attitude and has made steady progress in class. She is polite, attentive, and increasingly willing to try, especially when she is given a bit of time and support. Her reading skills are particularly strong, and she shows good understanding of the content covered in class. To continue improving, she should focus on speaking more confidently, using English as much as possible, and revising vocabulary and grammar regularly. With continued encouragement and practice, Emma is well placed to make further progress next term.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Behaviour, Work, Performance) plus Marks and a Final comment, as shown in the examples. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. All observations must be grounded in the source notes. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections (Behaviour/Work/Performance) + Marks + Final comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - tone: constructive and honest, matches examples - balance: weaknesses (pronunciation errors, reliance on French, avoiding speaking) acknowledged alongside strengths

response.output:
```html

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

--- Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Emma
Level: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good
Comment: Emma is generally attentive, polite, and respectful in class. She actively participates in discussions when given time and asks questions when unsure. She maintains a positive attitude and contributes to a supportive classroom environment, although she sometimes avoids speaking initially.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good
Comment: Emma demonstrates a solid commitment to her studies and participates actively in class activities. She handles grammar vocabulary and target language structures with competence, though she occasionally needs reminders about specific grammatical rules. While she initially avoids speaking, she engages more when encouraged and shows willingness to make efforts. Her pronunciation and occasional tense mistakes are areas for improvement, but she corrects herself when prompted.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good
Comment: Emma performs well in reading tasks and demonstrates a good grasp of vocabulary and grammatical structures. She can summarize chapters, create questions, and answer using the target language effectively. Her ability to understand and participate in listening activities is strong, though pronunciation and fluency require ongoing practice.

Final comment

Emma has shown consistent dedication and a positive attitude in her learning. She actively participates in class discussions and contributes to a collaborative environment. While she excels in reading and understanding vocabulary, she should continue to work on pronunciation, fluency, and speaking confidence. With continued encouragement and practice, she is well-positioned to further develop her language skills.

```

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Emma
  • Class: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Very good

Comment: Emma is a polite and attentive student who maintains a positive attitude in class. She is respectful toward her teacher and classmates, though she occasionally appears uneasy. Her mood is generally good, and she engages well when given time and encouragement. She works calmly and collaborates effectively with peers like Romain and Helena, showing improved confidence when working in smaller groups.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good

Comment: Emma demonstrates effort and participation in class activities, particularly when given support or additional time. She engages well in collaborative tasks and shows willingness to improve, though she sometimes hesitates to attempt tasks independently. Her focus is strong when motivated, and she responds positively to encouragement. However, she occasionally avoids speaking or writing tasks, preferring to seek help or clarification—sometimes in French—when unsure. Her reading skills are impressive, and she applies grammatical structures correctly with reminders.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

Comment: Emma shows a solid understanding of vocabulary and grammatical structures, particularly when reviewing previous material. She performs well in listening and reading tasks, summarizing chapters and answering questions accurately. Her use of target language in sentences is improving, though she occasionally struggles with pronunciation, third-person singular forms, and tenses (e.g., using "do" instead of "does"). She can correct some mistakes when prompted and applies connectors like "would rather" and "prefer" effectively. Her writing reveals errors in punctuation and capitalization, and she sometimes relies on peers for vocabulary support.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: Not assessed this term.

Writing Mark: Not assessed this term.

Homework mark: Not assessed this term.

---

Final comment

Emma has made noticeable progress this term, particularly in her reading fluency and ability to apply grammatical structures with guidance. She is polite, attentive, and increasingly confident when working collaboratively. While she still hesitates to speak or write independently—sometimes defaulting to French for vocabulary—she responds well to encouragement and shows a growing willingness to expand her answers. To build on her strengths, Emma should focus on practicing pronunciation, revising tenses, and using English more consistently in class. With continued effort and self-assurance, she has the potential to achieve even greater accuracy and fluency.

Kids&Us.

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Emma
Teacher: Huryv Osmanova
Level: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday 2025/2026
Absences: 0

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent

Emma is a polite and well-mannered student who generally shows a positive attitude in class. She is calm, respectful, and attentive, and she works well with both her teacher and classmates. At times, she can be a little hesitant to speak up, but with encouragement she is willing to try and becomes more confident. She responds well when given time to think and can be very receptive to guidance. Her kind and cooperative nature contributes positively to the classroom atmosphere.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good

Emma takes part in class activities and usually completes the work she is asked to do with care. She has shown good effort throughout the term, especially when speaking and working with familiar structures. She can make sentences using the target language and is able to follow the lesson content well. However, she sometimes needs encouragement to try more independently and to avoid relying too much on French. Continuing to build confidence and expanding her vocabulary will help her participate more freely and consistently.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

Emma has made good progress this term. She understands the new vocabulary well and can recognise and use many of the structures covered in class. She is also able to read the stories and chapters well, and her reading is often impressive. In oral activities, she can answer using full structures and usually corrects herself when she makes mistakes, which is very positive. She still needs to work on pronunciation, connectors, and some grammar points such as the third person singular, pronouns, and tense accuracy. With regular practice and more confidence, she will continue to improve.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 7.10 Good.

Emma performs well orally when she feels comfortable and has enough time to think. She can repeat and use the target language accurately in many situations, and her pronunciation is generally improving. However, she still makes occasional pronunciation mistakes and sometimes needs support to expand her answers.

Writing Mark: 6.80 Good.

Emma's written work shows a good understanding of the language covered this term. She can complete grammar and vocabulary tasks quite well. However, she still makes some errors with capital letters, punctuation, and grammar when writing independently. Further practice with writing and careful revision before tests will help her gain more confidence and accuracy.

Homework mark: 6.50 Acceptable.

Emma has completed some homework and has generally shown a reasonable effort. To improve further, she should make a more consistent effort to practise at home, revise vocabulary regularly, and review the grammar points covered in class.

Final comment

Over the course of the term, Emma has shown a positive attitude and has made steady progress in class. She is polite, attentive, and increasingly willing to try, especially when she is given a bit of time and support. Her reading skills are particularly strong, and she shows good understanding of the content covered in class. To continue improving, she should focus on speaking more confidently, using English as much as possible, and revising vocabulary and grammar regularly. With continued encouragement and practice, Emma is well placed to make further progress next term.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Behaviour, Work, Performance) plus Marks and a Final comment, as shown in the examples. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. All observations must be grounded in the source notes. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections (Behaviour/Work/Performance) + Marks + Final comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - tone: constructive and honest, matches examples - balance: weaknesses (pronunciation errors, reliance on French, avoiding speaking) acknowledged alongside strengths "}]

response.output:
```html

Kids&Us.

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Emma
  • Class: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Very good

Comment: Emma is a polite and attentive student who maintains a positive attitude in class. She is respectful toward her teacher and classmates, though she occasionally appears uneasy. Her mood is generally good, and she engages well when given time and encouragement. She works calmly and collaborates effectively with peers like Romain and Helena, showing improved confidence when working in smaller groups.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good

Comment: Emma demonstrates effort and participation in class activities, particularly when given support or additional time. She engages well in collaborative tasks and shows willingness to improve, though she sometimes hesitates to attempt tasks independently. Her focus is strong when motivated, and she responds positively to encouragement. However, she occasionally avoids speaking or writing tasks, preferring to seek help or clarification—sometimes in French—when unsure. Her reading skills are impressive, and she applies grammatical structures correctly with reminders.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

Comment: Emma shows a solid understanding of vocabulary and grammatical structures, particularly when reviewing previous material. She performs well in listening and reading tasks, summarizing chapters and answering questions accurately. Her use of target language in sentences is improving, though she occasionally struggles with pronunciation, third-person singular forms, and tenses (e.g., using "do" instead of "does"). She can correct some mistakes when prompted and applies connectors like "would rather" and "prefer" effectively. Her writing reveals errors in punctuation and capitalization, and she sometimes relies on peers for vocabulary support.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: Not assessed this term.

Writing Mark: Not assessed this term.

Homework mark: Not assessed this term.

---

Final comment

Emma has made noticeable progress this term, particularly in her reading fluency and ability to apply grammatical structures with guidance. She is polite, attentive, and increasingly confident when working collaboratively. While she still hesitates to speak or write independently—sometimes defaulting to French for vocabulary—she responds well to encouragement and shows a growing willingness to expand her answers. To build on her strengths, Emma should focus on practicing pronunciation, revising tenses, and using English more consistently in class. With continued effort and self-assurance, she has the potential to achieve even greater accuracy and fluency.

```

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Emma
Teacher: Huryv Osmanova
Level: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday 2025/2026
Absences: 0

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent

Emma is a polite and well-mannered student who generally shows a positive attitude in class. She is calm, respectful, and attentive, and she works well with both her teacher and classmates. At times, she can be a little hesitant to speak up, but with encouragement she is willing to try and becomes more confident. She responds well when given time to think and can be very receptive to guidance. Her kind and cooperative nature contributes positively to the classroom atmosphere.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good

Emma takes part in class activities and usually completes the work she is asked to do with care. She has shown good effort throughout the term, especially when speaking and working with familiar structures. She can make sentences using the target language and is able to follow the lesson content well. However, she sometimes needs encouragement to try more independently and to avoid relying too much on French. Continuing to build confidence and expanding her vocabulary will help her participate more freely and consistently.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

Emma has made good progress this term. She understands the new vocabulary well and can recognise and use many of the structures covered in class. She is also able to read the stories and chapters well, and her reading is often impressive. In oral activities, she can answer using full structures and usually corrects herself when she makes mistakes, which is very positive. She still needs to work on pronunciation, connectors, and some grammar points such as the third person singular, pronouns, and tense accuracy. With regular practice and more confidence, she will continue to improve.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 7.10 Good.

Emma performs well orally when she feels comfortable and has enough time to think. She can repeat and use the target language accurately in many situations, and her pronunciation is generally improving. However, she still makes occasional pronunciation mistakes and sometimes needs support to expand her answers.

Writing Mark: 6.80 Good.

Emma's written work shows a good understanding of the language covered this term. She can complete grammar and vocabulary tasks quite well. However, she still makes some errors with capital letters, punctuation, and grammar when writing independently. Further practice with writing and careful revision before tests will help her gain more confidence and accuracy.

Homework mark: 6.50 Acceptable.

Emma has completed some homework and has generally shown a reasonable effort. To improve further, she should make a more consistent effort to practise at home, revise vocabulary regularly, and review the grammar points covered in class.

Final comment

Over the course of the term, Emma has shown a positive attitude and has made steady progress in class. She is polite, attentive, and increasingly willing to try, especially when she is given a bit of time and support. Her reading skills are particularly strong, and she shows good understanding of the content covered in class. To continue improving, she should focus on speaking more confidently, using English as much as possible, and revising vocabulary and grammar regularly. With continued encouragement and practice, Emma is well placed to make further progress next term.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Behaviour, Work, Performance) plus Marks and a Final comment, as shown in the examples. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. All observations must be grounded in the source notes. Kids&Us.

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Emma
Teacher: Huryv Osmanova
Level: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday 2025/2026
Absences: 0

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent

Emma is a polite and well-mannered student who generally shows a positive attitude in class. She is calm, respectful, and attentive, and she works well with both her teacher and classmates. At times, she can be a little hesitant to speak up, but with encouragement she is willing to try and becomes more confident. She responds well when given time to think and can be very receptive to guidance. Her kind and cooperative nature contributes positively to the classroom atmosphere.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good

Emma takes part in class activities and usually completes the work she is asked to do with care. She has shown good effort throughout the term, especially when speaking and working with familiar structures. She can make sentences using the target language and is able to follow the lesson content well. However, she sometimes needs encouragement to try more independently and to avoid relying too much on French. Continuing to build confidence and expanding her vocabulary will help her participate more freely and consistently.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

Emma has made good progress this term. She understands the new vocabulary well and can recognise and use many of the structures covered in class. She is also able to read the stories and chapters well, and her reading is often impressive. In oral activities, she can answer using full structures and usually corrects herself when she makes mistakes, which is very positive. She still needs to work on pronunciation, connectors, and some grammar points such as the third person singular, pronouns, and tense accuracy. With regular practice and more confidence, she will continue to improve.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 7.10 Good.

Emma performs well orally when she feels comfortable and has enough time to think. She can repeat and use the target language accurately in many situations, and her pronunciation is generally improving. However, she still makes occasional pronunciation mistakes and sometimes needs support to expand her answers.

Writing Mark: 6.80 Good.

Emma's written work shows a good understanding of the language covered this term. She can complete grammar and vocabulary tasks quite well. However, she still makes some errors with capital letters, punctuation, and grammar when writing independently. Further practice with writing and careful revision before tests will help her gain more confidence and accuracy.

Homework mark: 6.50 Acceptable.

Emma has completed some homework and has generally shown a reasonable effort. To improve further, she should make a more consistent effort to practise at home, revise vocabulary regularly, and review the grammar points covered in class.

Final comment

Over the course of the term, Emma has shown a positive attitude and has made steady progress in class. She is polite, attentive, and increasingly willing to try, especially when she is given a bit of time and support. Her reading skills are particularly strong, and she shows good understanding of the content covered in class. To continue improving, she should focus on speaking more confidently, using English as much as possible, and revising vocabulary and grammar regularly. With continued encouragement and practice, Emma is well placed to make further progress next term.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Behaviour, Work, Performance) plus Marks and a Final comment, as shown in the examples. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. All observations must be grounded in the source notes. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections (Behaviour/Work/Performance) + Marks + Final comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - tone: constructive and honest, matches examples - balance: weaknesses (pronunciation errors, reliance on French, avoiding speaking) acknowledged alongside strengths

[{"content":"You are a report card writer for a school teacher. The student notes are the sole source of facts for the report. Every observation, data field, and mark must come from the notes — not from any examples. ## Style & Layout Guide The following are example report cards. Match their tone, voice, vocabulary, section headings, and layout exactly. These examples may differ slightly from the provided additional instructions - in that case, the instructions from the user take precedence. IMPORTANT: Do not copy specific Data field values, Marks, or observations from the examples — only include a Data field or Marks section if that value is present in the student notes. ### Example 1: Informes 3r trimestre Alice Ling Excellent.pdf Kids&Us. COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 1, 3rd term --- Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Alice Ling Teacher: Huryv Osmanova Level: Tweens 3 2024/2025 Absences: 0 Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent Comment: Alice continues to be a kind, responsible, and respectful student. She is always polite and considerate towards both her classmates and teachers. She actively listens, engages in class discussions, and consistently asks for clarification when she doesn't understand something. Her positive and cooperative nature contributes to a welcoming and supportive classroom environment. Work Assessment (Work): Excellent Comment: Alice remains an engaged and hardworking student. She participates actively in class and completes her tasks with enthusiasm. She consistently takes initiative in her learning and demonstrates great responsibility. However, she should make an effort to revise more regularly and complete all assigned homework to reinforce her understanding of the material. Performance Assessment (Performance): Excellent Comment: Alice continues to excel in her academic performance. She has achieved excellent results in her assessments and demonstrates a strong grasp of vocabulary, grammar, and comprehension. Her test results are consistently impressive, and she shows great confidence in both oral and written tasks. Marks Oral Test Mark: 10.00 Excellent. Writing Mark: 9.80 Excellent. Homework mark: 0.00 Needs to improve. --- Final comment Alice has proven to be a bright, motivated, and dedicated student. She consistently demonstrates a strong command of English and maintains excellent behaviour and participation in class. With improved consistency in completing homework and regular revision, she is well-positioned to continue excelling. ### Example 2: Informes 3r trimestre Romain Fafchamps Good.pdf Kids&Us. COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 1, 3rd term --- Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Romain Fachamps Teacher: Huryu Osmanova Level: Tweens 3 2024/2025 Absences: 0 Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent Comment: Romain continues to be a kind, respectful, and responsible student. He consistently maintains a positive attitude in class and creates a welcoming atmosphere for both classmates and the teacher. He is cooperative, listens attentively, and asks questions when unsure. Work Assessment (Work): Good Comment: Romain works diligently and remains committed to his studies. He actively participates in discussions and approaches learning with curiosity. When facing difficulties, he takes the initiative to seek clarification. Performance Assessment (Performance): Good Comment: Romain performed well in assessments, showing a solid understanding of the material. He continues to expand his vocabulary and improve sentence structure. Focusing on revising vocabulary and grammar will enhance his accuracy. Marks Oral Test Mark: 7.20 Good. Writing Mark: 7.90 Good. Homework mark: 6.50 Acceptable. --- Final comment Over the course of the year, Romain has shown consistent dedication and grown in confidence, particularly in speaking and writing. His positive attitude and respectful nature benefit the classroom environment. To continue his progress over the summer, he should focus on regular reading, writing practice, and speaking activities. ### Example 3: Informes 3r trimestre Ylena Salmon Excellent FT2.pdf Kids&Us. COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 1, 3rd term --- Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Ylena Salmon Teacher: Huruv Osmanova Level: Tweens 3 2024/2025 Absences: 0 Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent Comment: Ylena continues to be a kind, responsible, and respectful student. She always comes to class with a positive attitude and maintains a strong connection with both her teacher and classmates. She is attentive, asks questions when she does not understand something, and actively engages in discussions. Work Assessment (Work): Very good Comment: Ylena is a hardworking student who consistently participates in class activities. She is always engaged in discussions, actively contributing to group work and classroom exercises. She demonstrates responsibility in her learning and asks for clarification whenever needed. Performance Assessment (Performance): Excellent Comment: Ylena performed very well in her assessments. She is a confident speaker and expresses her thoughts clearly. She has a strong grasp of vocabulary and sentence structures but should continue to revise certain grammar points for greater accuracy. Marks Oral Test Mark: 9.40 Excellent. Writing Mark: 8.70 Very good. Homework mark: 0.00 Needs to improve. --- Final comment Ylena has shown consistent dedication throughout the year. She maintains a positive attitude, shows respect to her peers and teacher, and takes initiative in her learning. Her strong speaking and writing skills reflect her overall engagement. With improved homework habits and continued focus on grammar and vocabulary, she has great potential to reach excellence across all areas. ## Student Notes (source of truth — all report content must derive from these) Student: Emma, Class: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday - 2026-05-14: Emma came very late, 25 minutes late, and she left early. She was in a rather good mood, but she didn't understand some of the vocabulary, so she asked me to tell some of the words in French. She looked a bit desperate because she didn't understand what was going on, so she was a bit upset about that. When she saw that it was a writing task, she looked very happy not to have to do it because she was leaving at that time. But she did the grammar vocabulary very well, and she only needed a little reminder that after used to, we had to use a verb in -ing. - 2026-05-14: Emma was the only student who came to class because Alice, Romain and Hélène were absent. The class went really well: she was much more confident, could take her time to answer, and looked very comfortable asking me questions. She is more willing to make efforts and try to get the right answers when I give her more time or do not give her the answers straight away. She was very attentive and polite, like always, and she read the text quite well; her reading was really, really impressive. She still sometimes makes errors when she pronounces some words, but I told her I found her very good today and that I was very happy and proud of her. - 2026-05-14: Emma could use some of the grammatical structures and remembered a little bit of the previous chapters. I was quite happy with her work, and she was quite serious with it. She was also quite happy and in a focused, happy mood, even though she was a bit playful with Elena. - 2026-05-14: Emma took part in the activities quite well and could use the target language in the gapped sentences quite well. She was trying to avoid speaking as much as possible, and when I asked her to speak, she did. She was a bit on the defensive side about the writing task, said she would do it if she had time, and she sometimes asks for vocabulary words in French because she lacks them. She also sometimes makes mistakes when she speaks, especially with pronouns, and sometimes forgets tenses or the third-person singular -s. - 2026-05-14: Helena helped her a lot with the vocabulary. Emma still asks sometimes in French or asks for vocabulary words because she lacks them, so she doesn't know them. - 2026-05-14: Emma was more comfortable with Romain, and so she did the oral exercises well too. She still makes some mistakes, and she can usually correct them, so that's great. I was really proud of her. - 2026-05-14: Emma needed help because she couldn't remember what old-fashioned meant. They could do the listening activity quite well and could report what the characters had said quite well, although I needed to tell Emma to mind the 's' at the 3rd person singular. I helped Emma with her My Talent task. She couldn't spell 'teen' but I asked Romain to help her and he did. - 2026-05-14: Emma was calm and attentive and did the work she was asked as well as she could. She could read the chapter quite well, without pronunciation mistakes, but she was not totally fluent. She could ask for words she did not understand, though she asked them in French to Ylena. She could summarize the chapters using the target language written on the board rather well, make questions from the target language, answer using it too, and understand a song and answer questions about it. She could form a question and an answer using "would rather…than…" and "prefer… to…". She seemed a bit uneasy with me, but it got better by the end. She could not really use vocabulary words and make sentences with them that showed understanding of the words. - 2026-05-14: They could all express themselves rather well when talking about food groups, dishes and meals. They could use the structures given by the teacher very well, and they worked on an argumentative text quite well. When we worked collaboratively, Emma participated, but when it was individual, she asked to go to the loo and stayed there a bit. Emma and Romain needed a bit of help for inspiration, and Emma sometimes needed a bit of help with pronunciation. She knew how to use the connector but had to be reminded of its purpose. - 2026-05-14: Emma made some efforts to talk a bit more and expand her answers, being more confident. Romain and Emma had forgotten how to use connectors such as although and despite. Emma still makes some pronunciation mistakes and sometimes makes tense mistakes like using "do" for "he/she." The class could do the exercises quite well and understand the new vocabulary very well too, and they all did an effort to express themselves. - 2026-05-14: The group went well overall: they were all polite, motivated, and interested. They could remember parts of the story quite well, answer using full structures, understand the vocabulary well, and do the games well too. Emma struggled a bit with pronunciation, but could repeat the correct words. She does not always try and says that she does not know, so I need to encourage her to try, which she will sometimes do. She asked for something to Alice in French, and I told her to ask in English, which she did. In the My Talent Body 1 part, she made mistakes mainly about capital letters and punctuation. - 2026-05-14: The Fairy Tales 2 students were all motivated. They could all do the exercises well enough, make sentences with a comparative, understand and hear the mistakes in the songs, and read very well. Emma was sometimes making a couple of mistakes with the pronunciation. - 2026-05-22: Emma could use the causative form (get something done) correctly. She could create a problem and give a solution using the target language. She could review the target language by using words from the previous chapters in a sentence. She could turn nouns into adjectives. She seemed less comfortable giving out full answers today. She could find vocabulary words in a text and answer questions about her reading. ## Task Write a report card narrative for this student based on the notes above. Kids&Us.

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Emma
Teacher: [Not specified in notes]
Level: Fairy Tales 2
Class: Tuesday & Friday

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good
Comment: Emma is generally polite, attentive, and motivated in class. She maintains a good mood and shows respect toward her classmates and teacher. She sometimes appears uneasy but becomes more comfortable as the class progresses. She is cooperative and willing to participate when encouraged.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good
Comment: Emma engages in class activities and demonstrates effort in her work. She is serious when focused and can complete tasks well, though she occasionally avoids speaking or writing tasks. She benefits from additional time and encouragement to try. She participates in collaborative work but sometimes seeks distractions during individual tasks.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good
Comment: Emma shows a solid understanding of grammar and vocabulary, though she sometimes struggles with pronunciation, tenses (e.g., third-person singular -s), and connectors. She can read well and summarize chapters using target language. She performs better in oral exercises when comfortable and can correct some of her own mistakes. Her writing shows occasional errors with capitalization and punctuation.

Marks

[No marks provided in notes]

---

Final comment

Emma is a motivated and polite student who demonstrates a good grasp of the material. She reads impressively and engages well in collaborative activities. With continued encouragement to speak and write more confidently, and with practice on pronunciation, tenses, and connectors, she can further improve her accuracy and fluency. Her willingness to ask questions and seek clarification when unsure is a strength.

Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Emma course, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Chloé
Teacher: Emma Saturday
Level: Emma 2024/2025

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good
Comment:

Chloé participates enthusiastically in all the activities. She enjoys story time and can repeat the story with her classmates and her teacher. Chloé also shows a real interest in the worksheet activities, the "what's missing" activity, and the new story tattoo activity. She is always cooperative with her teacher and her classmates.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good
Comment:

Chloé's English knowledge is good. She knows a great amount of vocabulary and structures. She can often use them in sentences, sometimes requiring the teacher's help to do so. This term, she specifically learnt the different actions and the different rooms/places in which those actions take place (e.g.: sleep in the bedroom, eat in the kitchen, etc.). She has also reinforced her knowledge of the shapes, the colours, the moods, the body parts and the food items. Chloé can pair up Emma's clean/dirty body parts, repeat the right structures, and place tattoos on the correct body parts with a little help. She can also tell her age and gender using the complete structure.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good
Comment:

Chloé is a joyful and attentive student. She treats her classmates and her teacher with respect and care. She listens attentively in class and usually stays focused during the lesson, though she can be distracted at times. On a few occasions, she asked repetitive questions, which annoyed her classmates, but she stopped when reminded. Chloé takes good care of the material and shares it with her classmates.

Final comment

Throughout the school year, Chloé has shown great enthusiasm and cooperation in class. She has made significant progress in her English learning, developing both her memory and her comprehension skills. It is recommended to reinforce the vocabulary and structures learnt throughout the school year to ensure continued improvement.

Objectives and content of the Emma course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Chloé
  • Teacher: [Teacher]
  • Level: Emma Saturday 2025/2026

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good

Comment:
Chloé usually comes to class in a good mood and is generally willing to take part in the activities proposed. She is open to new tasks and, once reminded of the classroom routine, she joins in with enthusiasm. At the beginning of some sessions, she could be a little loud or repeatedly ask for confirmation, but she usually settles well when given clear guidance. Chloé is cooperative with the teacher and with her classmates, and she especially enjoys hands-on activities such as worksheet work, bingo, cards and dressing Emma.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Satisfactory

Comment:
Chloé's English knowledge is satisfactory. She can repeat the new structures well and often understands the language being worked on in class. This term, she worked on colours, clothes, body parts, actions, rooms and places, as well as the concepts of clean/dirty and dry/wet. She can usually tell her age and gender and, on several occasions, used the complete structure correctly. She was also able to pair actions with the correct rooms and take part in the story-time activities. However, she still needs more practice with some vocabulary items, especially clothes and colours, and she sometimes needs the teacher's help to remember or apply the full structure. With more regular revision, she will continue to gain confidence and accuracy.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

Comment:
Chloé is a lively and generally well-integrated student. She can be a little distracted or impatient at times, especially when she is tired or when the group is especially active, but she responds well to reminders and class management strategies. Once the rules are clear, she behaves appropriately and stays focused on the task. She usually treats the material carefully and works well with the rest of the group. Her attitude in class is positive and she is able to adapt well to the pace of the lesson.

Final comment

Throughout the term, Chloé has shown a positive attitude towards the classroom routines and has taken part well in the activities. She is able to understand and repeat a good part of the language worked on in class, and she is becoming more confident when using complete structures. She still needs to keep reinforcing some vocabulary, especially clothes and colours, but she is making good progress. Well done, Chloé! It is recommended to keep listening to the audio tracks and revising the vocabulary at home in order to consolidate the language learnt during the term.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Participation/Motivation, Learning process, Behaviour) plus a Final comment, as shown in the reference. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. The report should synthesise observations from multiple notes into a cohesive narrative. IMPORTANT: Do not invent Data fields (Absences, Frequency of use, Teacher name, Oral Test Mark, or other numerical/structured metadata) — only include facts traceable to the source notes. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections + Final comment, each with Assessment and Comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations, no fabricated marks, absences counts, or audio-frequency values) - synthesis: observations from multiple notes woven together, not a note-by-note list - tone: warm, encouraging, matches Emma-level examples "}]

IMPORTANT: Do not invent Data fields (Absences, Frequency of use, Teacher name, Oral Test Mark, or other numerical/structured metadata) — only include facts traceable to the source notes. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections + Final comment, each with Assessment and Comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations, no fabricated marks, absences counts, or audio-frequency values) - synthesis: observations from multiple notes woven together, not a note-by-note list - tone: warm, encouraging, matches Emma-level examples

[{"content":"You are a report card writer for a school teacher. The student notes are the sole source of facts for the report. Every observation, data field, and mark must come from the notes — not from any examples. ## Style & Layout Guide The following are example report cards. Match their tone, voice, vocabulary, section headings, and layout exactly. These examples may differ slightly from the provided additional instructions - in that case, the instructions from the user take precedence. IMPORTANT: Do not copy specific Data field values, Marks, or observations from the examples — only include a Data field or Marks section if that value is present in the student notes. ### Example 1: Informes 3r trimestre Camille Martin null.pdf Kids&Us. Objectives and content of the Emma course, 3rd term --- Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Camille Martin Teacher: Christine Blois Level: Emma 2024/2025 Absences: 2 Frequency of use (audio): Insufficient Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good Comment: Camille comes to class in a good mood and a smile on her face. She participates enthusiastically in all the activities. Camille truly loves story time. She likes reciting the story with her classmates and her teacher. Camille also really enjoys the "Numbers song", the "Can you please...?", the "Happy face/sad face card game" and the "Where is Emma's tattoo?" activities. Camille loves singing along to the songs and she also shows a real interest in the worksheet activities. Camille is always cooperative with her teacher and her classmates. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Good Comment: Camille's English knowledge is good. She knows a great amount of vocabulary and structures. She can often use them in sentences, sometimes requiring the teacher's help to do so. This term, she specifically learnt the different actions and the different rooms/places in which those actions take place (e.g.: sleep in the bedroom, eat in the kitchen, etc.). She has also reinforced her knowledge of the shapes, the colours, the moods, the body parts and the food items. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Very good Comment: Camille is a joyful, attentive, friendly and bright student. She treats her classmates and her teacher with respect and care. She listens attentively in class and usually stays focused during the lesson, seldom being distracted by her classmates. On a few occasions, she jokingly kept the material instead of giving it back to the teacher, but usually, gives it back directly when asked. Moreover, she takes good care of the material and always shares it with her classmates. Marks Oral Test Mark: 7.20 Good. Camille can understand the question and answers either with a single word or with a structure which is only partially correct. The decrease in her audio listening frequency might explain her good result, instead of a very good one! The teacher still wants to congratulate Camille on her good result! Keep up the good work! --- Final comment At the beginning of the school year, Camille was a very quiet and shy student. Thanks to her classmates’ friendliness and her easy-going attitude, she came out of her shell and has felt comfortable in the class ever since. It has truly been a joy to have Camille in class, thanks to her smiling and her good-humoured attitude. Furthermore, she has also made progress in her English learning, developing both her memory and her comprehension skills. It is recommended to listen to the audio tracks during the summer holidays, in order to keep in mind the vocabulary and structures, learnt throughout the school year. ### Example 2: Informes 3r trimestre Luis Moreau Very Good Emma.pdf Kids&Us. Objectives and content of the Emma course, 3rd term --- Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Luis Moreau Teacher: Christine Blois Level: Emma 2024/2025 Absences: 0 Frequency of use (audio): Pass Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good Comment: Luis usually comes to class in a good and calm mood. He usually participates in the activities with a certain enthusiasm. Luis really likes story time and he takes an active role in the storytelling, reciting the story with the teacher. He also seemed to really enjoy the "Can you please...?", the "Happy face/sad face card game", the "Where can Emma...?" and the "Where can you...?" activities. Luis is always cooperative with his classmates and his teacher. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Very good Comment: Luis' English knowledge is very good. He knows a great amount of vocabulary and structures. He can often use them in sentences, sometimes requiring the teacher's help to do so. This term, he specifically learnt the different actions and the different rooms/places in which those actions take place (e.g. sleep in the bedroom, eat in the kitchen, etc.). He has also reinforced his knowledge of the shapes, the colours, the moods, the body parts and the food items. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Very good Comment: Luis is a composed, gentle, friendly and attentive student. He always treats his classmates and his teacher with respect and care. He is seldom distracted by his classmates or external elements. He usually stays focused and listens attentively, following the teacher's instructions very well. Luis, always treats the material with care, gives the material back directly when asked, and shares it with his classmates. Marks Oral Test Mark: 8.40 Very good. Luis can understand the question and makes an effort to use a complete structure in the answer, even if it is not always the correct one. Congratulations on your very good result, Luis! We're very proud of you! --- Final comment Throughout the year, Luis has opened up and has seemed to really feel more comfortable with his classmates. It is a real joy to have Luis in class, thanks to his diligent and mindful attitude, as well as his friendliness. Moreover, Luis kept developing both his memory and comprehension skills. His oral test results have been in constant progress. Well done, Luis! Furthermore, in order to keep in mind the vocabulary and structures learnt throughout the school year, it is recommended to listen to the audio track during the summer holidays. ### Example 3: Informes 3r trimestre Rayan Bernard de Fortemps Acceptable Emma.pdf Kids&Us. Objectives and content of the Emma course, 3rd term --- Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Rayan Bernard Teacher: Christine Blois Level: Emma 2024/2025 Absences: 0 Frequency of use (audio): Insufficient Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good Comment: Rayan usually comes to class in a cheerful and lively mood. He participates in the activities with great enthusiasm. Rayan does not seem overly interested in story time, usually losing focus during that activity. However, he really enjoyed the "Can you please...?", the "Happy face/sad face card game", the "Where is Emma's tattoo?", the "Where can Emma...?" and the "Where can you...?" activities. Rayan is usually quite cooperative with his classmates and his teacher. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Satisfactory Comment: Rayan's English knowledge is satisfactory. He does know a certain amount of vocabulary and structures. However, he usually requires the teacher's help to repeat the full structures and tell him how he must use words in sentences. This term he specifically learnt the different actions and the different rooms/places in which those actions take place (e.g.: sleep in the bedroom, eat in the kitchen, etc.). He has also reinforced his knowledge of the shapes, the colours, the moods, the body parts and the food items. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Good Comment: Rayan is a fun-loving, energetic and good-humoured student. He seldom treats his classmates in an unfriendly manner, generally treating them with respect and care. He also generally treats his teacher in a respectful manner. Rayan can still, at times, be distracted by external elements and his classmates. However, he has learnt to stay more focused and listen more attentively to the teacher, following the teacher's instructions rather well. He tends to treat the material with care and can give it back directly when asked, and usually shares it with his classmates. Marks Oral Test Mark: 6.20 Acceptable. Rayan can understand the question but, in most cases, still does not respond with a complete structure. The teacher had noticed a real improvement in Rayan's learning, when Rayan's audio listening frequency was more regular. He was able to understand what was expected of him during the activities and the meaning of the words used in the activities. Keep up the good work, Rayan! --- Final comment Throughout the school year and especially this term, Rayan’s behaviour has really improved. He still needs to move around and explore his surroundings, but he does so in a calmer way. He has been getting better at listening to the teacher and staying more focused. He has kept his grades up and with some work and a regular audio listening, his English level can only increase! It is recommended to listen to the audio tracks during the summer holidays, in order to keep in mind the vocabulary and structures learnt throughout the year. ## Important Additional Instructions from User - Takes precedence over any provided example MOTIVATION AND PARTICIPATION: o Motivation: definition: factor or group of factors that bring about a specific type of behaviour. This section should comment on whether the student shows a positive attitude towards different activities conducted in the classroom: ▪ How does the student come into the classroom? ▪ Do they show willingness to learn? ▪ Are they open to doing new activities? ▪ Are they cooperative with the rest of the group and with the teacher? o Participation: definition: involvement in an event, act or activity. Parents must be informed whether their child participates in the activities that are carried out in the classroom and whether or not they put previously-acquired knowledge, as well as language currently being worked on, into practice. ▪ Outline the degree of participation based on what the student views as being important. For example, he/she is very competitive and always wants to win. He/she gets cross when they don't win. He/she always wants to come first. ▪ Another noteworthy aspect is whether the student holds back when new material is introduced, and whether they take part to a lesser degree than when internalised knowledge is elicited in class. BEHAVIOUR: definition: the way in which a person behaves in a specific or general situation. o Note whether the student is integrated within the group or whether they are easily distracted or unable to focus. o Point out, if necessary, how a student is reacting in light of situations that have had to be tackled with a specific plan. o Explain whether the environment or general mood of the group, or the student's particular mood, affects the student's behaviour. Is the group more easily distracted than normal? Is the student especially tired on rainy days, after public holidays, etc.? 14 LEARNING: definition: the process of studying, practising or experiencing whereby an individual or a group acquires specific knowledge, particularly knowledge required for learning an art form or profession. o Use of structures worked on during the story o Level of learning objectives achieved o Strengths o Weaknesses, together with a plan for improvement and commitment on behalf of the school to work on this aspect. Avoid repetitions in the sections ## Student Notes (source of truth — all report content must derive from these) Student: Chloé, Class: Emma Saturday - 2026-05-14: Chloé did not know the colours or the clothes.
