Is the group more easily distracted than normal? Is the student especially tired on rainy days, after public holidays, etc.? 14 LEARNING: definition: the process of studying, practising or experiencing whereby an individual or a group acquires specific knowledge, particularly knowledge required for learning an art form or profession. o Use of structures worked on during the story o Level of learning objectives achieved o Strengths o Weaknesses, together with a plan for improvement and commitment on behalf of the school to work on this aspect. Avoid repetitions in the same section. Keep an encouraging and positive tone Reference report (a known-good output for the same notes — use as a quality benchmark, student name may differ):

Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Mila
  • Teacher: Thérèse Garnier
  • Level: Oliver Thursday 2025/2026
  • Period: 2026-02-22 to 2026-05-22

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment: Mila generally comes to class in a positive mood and shows interest in the activities presented. She is usually willing to participate and can be very engaged when she feels comfortable with the task, especially in action-based activities and games. At times, however, she can become upset when things do not go as she expects, which affects her willingness to continue taking part. With encouragement, she is able to rejoin the class and show good motivation, particularly when she has the opportunity to help or demonstrate what she knows.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment: Mila has shown good understanding of the structures and vocabulary worked on this term. She can repeat the target language well and, in many activities, uses the new language with confidence. Her knowledge of the story has improved notably, and she is able to retell parts of it with little support. She also performs very well in activities involving weather, clothing, and colour vocabulary. At times, she still needs help with some specific words and routines, such as taking off and putting on, and she may need further practice to remain consistent when new language is introduced. Continued repetition and reinforcement will help her consolidate her progress even further.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

Comment: Mila is well integrated in the group and can participate positively in class. On the whole, she behaves appropriately and works well with the rest of the students, especially during practical and familiar activities. However, when she becomes frustrated or does not get her way, she may withdraw, refuse to participate, or show signs of anger, which can affect her focus during the lesson. She sometimes needs reminders to stay engaged and remain seated during story time. When she is calm, she responds well and shows a much more cooperative attitude.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 8.00    Good. Mila can understand the question and answers either with a single word or with a structure which is only partially correct.

Final comment

Mila has made good progress this term and has shown that she can understand and use a great deal of the language worked on in class. Her ability to repeat structures, recognise vocabulary, and take part in action activities is very promising. With continued encouragement to manage moments of frustration and to stay engaged throughout the whole lesson, she will be able to keep building confidence and continue developing her English skills successfully.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Participation/Motivation, Learning process, Behaviour) plus Marks and a Final comment, as shown in the examples. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. The notes include moments of frustration and withdrawal — the report should handle these honestly while maintaining an encouraging tone. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections + Marks + Final comment, each with Assessment and Comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - tone: encouraging and positive while honest about challenges - behaviour: frustration and withdrawal handled constructively, not ignored "}]

Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Mila
Teacher: Thérèse Garnier
Level: Oliver 2024/2025

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment:

Mila demonstrates a positive attitude towards classroom activities, arriving in a happy mood and showing willingness to participate. She actively engages in tasks, such as repeating structures and using vocabulary, though she occasionally closes up when things do not go her way. Mila is cooperative within the group and enjoys contributing to activities like the Dice Game and Family Bingo. However, she sometimes struggles with setbacks, such as not being able to see the Big Book or not getting her way, which can momentarily affect her participation.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:

Mila has shown strong progress in acquiring vocabulary and structures, particularly in weather routines and storytelling. She can combine moods with actions well and understands how to use new vocabulary in context. Mila excels in retelling stories and repeating structures, though she occasionally needs support with specific phrases like "taking off" and "putting on." Her ability to pair sentences with realia items and complete worksheets demonstrates her grasp of the material. However, she sometimes disengages when frustrated, such as refusing to participate in certain activities after not getting her way.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

Comment:

Mila generally behaves well in class and is integrated within the group. She participates enthusiastically in activities like the present activity and enjoys helping distribute materials. However, she can become upset when things do not go as planned, such as when she cannot see the Big Book or is not allowed to keep blu-tack. These moments of frustration sometimes lead her to lay down or turn her back during story time, though she responds well to reminders to sit properly. Overall, her behaviour improves when she is engaged and happy with the activities.

Final comment

________________________________________________________________________________

Mila has shown commendable progress this term, particularly in her vocabulary acquisition and ability to use structures in context. Her enthusiasm for learning is evident, though she occasionally faces challenges with frustration when things do not go her way. Encouraging her to manage these emotions will help her continue to grow. With continued practice and a positive approach, Mila is well on her way to further developing her language skills. I look forward to seeing her progress in the coming year.

Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Mila

Level: Oliver Thursday

Participation/Motivation

Comment:

Mila comes to class in a very happy mood and shows willingness to take part in the activities. She enjoys helping hand out the material, is happy to participate in action activities, and joins in well with games such as the present activity, Family Bingo and the Dice Game. When she does not get her way, she can close up a little, but she generally settles once she is engaged in the lesson.

Learning process

Comment:

Mila knows most of the vocabulary, structures, songs, gestures and story, and she can tell the story with almost no help. She repeats the structures very well and already understands how to use the new vocabulary words during activities. She combines the mood and the actions very well, knows the phrase that goes with her weather card, and can pair the weather and clothing item combination very well. She also worked very well on the worksheet activity and showed that she knew the colour vocabulary, including white and black. She sometimes needs help with the words “taking off” and “putting on” during the weather routines.

Behaviour

Comment:

Mila is integrated within the group and can take part positively, but when she becomes upset her behaviour is affected. On some occasions she only listened at first and did not take part in the routines straight away. She was also upset when she could not see the Big Book during story time, and she laid down a few times, was asked to sit, and turned her back during the whole story. At times she refused to join activities such as True or False and the Dice Game, but once she settles she can participate well again.

Final comment

________________________________________________________________________________

Mila has shown strong understanding and use of the course language, and she can take part very well when she is settled. Her enthusiasm is clear in many activities, and her ability to repeat structures and tell the story is a strength. With continued support in managing frustration and in the weather routine words she still needs help with, she can keep building confidence and continue to make progress.

Is the group more easily distracted than normal? Is the student especially tired on rainy days, after public holidays, etc.? 14 LEARNING: definition: the process of studying, practising or experiencing whereby an individual or a group acquires specific knowledge, particularly knowledge required for learning an art form or profession. o Use of structures worked on during the story o Level of learning objectives achieved o Strengths o Weaknesses, together with a plan for improvement and commitment on behalf of the school to work on this aspect. Avoid repetitions in the same section. Keep an encouraging and positive tone Reference report (a known-good output for the same notes — use as a quality benchmark, student name may differ):

Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Mila
  • Teacher: Thérèse Garnier
  • Level: Oliver Thursday 2025/2026
  • Period: 2026-02-22 to 2026-05-22

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment: Mila generally comes to class in a positive mood and shows interest in the activities presented. She is usually willing to participate and can be very engaged when she feels comfortable with the task, especially in action-based activities and games. At times, however, she can become upset when things do not go as she expects, which affects her willingness to continue taking part. With encouragement, she is able to rejoin the class and show good motivation, particularly when she has the opportunity to help or demonstrate what she knows.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment: Mila has shown good understanding of the structures and vocabulary worked on this term. She can repeat the target language well and, in many activities, uses the new language with confidence. Her knowledge of the story has improved notably, and she is able to retell parts of it with little support. She also performs very well in activities involving weather, clothing, and colour vocabulary. At times, she still needs help with some specific words and routines, such as taking off and putting on, and she may need further practice to remain consistent when new language is introduced. Continued repetition and reinforcement will help her consolidate her progress even further.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

Comment: Mila is well integrated in the group and can participate positively in class. On the whole, she behaves appropriately and works well with the rest of the students, especially during practical and familiar activities. However, when she becomes frustrated or does not get her way, she may withdraw, refuse to participate, or show signs of anger, which can affect her focus during the lesson. She sometimes needs reminders to stay engaged and remain seated during story time. When she is calm, she responds well and shows a much more cooperative attitude.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 8.00    Good. Mila can understand the question and answers either with a single word or with a structure which is only partially correct.

Final comment

Mila has made good progress this term and has shown that she can understand and use a great deal of the language worked on in class. Her ability to repeat structures, recognise vocabulary, and take part in action activities is very promising. With continued encouragement to manage moments of frustration and to stay engaged throughout the whole lesson, she will be able to keep building confidence and continue developing her English skills successfully.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Participation/Motivation, Learning process, Behaviour) plus Marks and a Final comment, as shown in the examples. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. The notes include moments of frustration and withdrawal — the report should handle these honestly while maintaining an encouraging tone. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections + Marks + Final comment, each with Assessment and Comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - tone: encouraging and positive while honest about challenges - behaviour: frustration and withdrawal handled constructively, not ignored

response.output:
Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Mila

Level: Oliver Thursday

Participation/Motivation

Comment:

Mila comes to class in a very happy mood and shows willingness to take part in the activities. She enjoys helping hand out the material, is happy to participate in action activities, and joins in well with games such as the present activity, Family Bingo and the Dice Game. When she does not get her way, she can close up a little, but she generally settles once she is engaged in the lesson.

Learning process

Comment:

Mila knows most of the vocabulary, structures, songs, gestures and story, and she can tell the story with almost no help. She repeats the structures very well and already understands how to use the new vocabulary words during activities. She combines the mood and the actions very well, knows the phrase that goes with her weather card, and can pair the weather and clothing item combination very well. She also worked very well on the worksheet activity and showed that she knew the colour vocabulary, including white and black. She sometimes needs help with the words “taking off” and “putting on” during the weather routines.

Behaviour

Comment:

Mila is integrated within the group and can take part positively, but when she becomes upset her behaviour is affected. On some occasions she only listened at first and did not take part in the routines straight away. She was also upset when she could not see the Big Book during story time, and she laid down a few times, was asked to sit, and turned her back during the whole story. At times she refused to join activities such as True or False and the Dice Game, but once she settles she can participate well again.

Final comment

________________________________________________________________________________

Mila has shown strong understanding and use of the course language, and she can take part very well when she is settled. Her enthusiasm is clear in many activities, and her ability to repeat structures and tell the story is a strength. With continued support in managing frustration and in the weather routine words she still needs help with, she can keep building confidence and continue to make progress.

Grounding is mixed: many learning details are traceable to the notes, but some claims are overgeneralized or not clearly supported, such as consistently strong enthusiasm, showing respect for peers/classmates, contributing to a collaborative environment, and responding positively to gentle reminders. The ratings of 'Very good' for participation and learning also seem somewhat inflated given the documented refusals, withdrawal, and need for help with some routines. Overall, it is partially aligned but misses key structural requirements and has some unsupported statements.

Term report (3rd term)

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good

Comment:

  • Mila consistently shows enthusiasm and a positive attitude towards learning, actively engaging in classroom activities.
  • She demonstrates a willingness to participate and apply her knowledge of vocabulary and structures during lessons.
  • Mila’s participation is particularly strong during storytelling and vocabulary exercises, where she contributes confidently.
  • She occasionally closes up when her expectations are not met, such as when she could not see the Big Book or keep the blu-tack, but she regains composure and participates again.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Very good

Comment:

  • Mila has a strong grasp of vocabulary, structures, songs, gestures, and the story content.
  • She effectively combines mood and actions, demonstrating a solid understanding of the material.
  • Mila can retell parts of the story with minimal assistance and uses new vocabulary appropriately during activities.
  • Her ability to pair sentences with weather items and combine clothing items with colors showcases her comprehension and application of learned concepts.
  • She quickly recalls and uses newly introduced vocabulary and structures during activities.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

Comment:

  • Mila generally exhibits positive behavior in the classroom, interacting well with peers and engaging in group activities.
  • She shows respect for the teacher and classmates, contributing to a collaborative environment.
  • Mila has moments of frustration, such as laying down, turning her back during story time, or closing up when upset, but she responds positively to gentle reminders and guidance.
  • Her behavior improves once she regains composure, and she actively participates in subsequent activities.
  • During activities like the Dice Game and Family Bingo, she participates enthusiastically once engaged.

Final comment

________________________________________________________________________________

Mila has made excellent progress this term, demonstrating a strong command of vocabulary and structures. Her enthusiasm and willingness to participate in activities are commendable, even when faced with challenges. While she occasionally experiences moments of frustration, her overall behavior and engagement contribute positively to the classroom environment. Continued encouragement and practice will help her further develop her skills and confidence. Well done, Mila!

Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Mila
  • Teacher: Thérèse Garnier
  • Level: Oliver Thursday 2025/2026
  • Period: 2026-02-22 to 2026-05-22

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment: Mila generally comes to class in a positive mood and shows interest in the activities presented. She is usually willing to participate and can be very engaged when she feels comfortable with the task, especially in action-based activities and games. At times, however, she can become upset when things do not go as she expects, which affects her willingness to continue taking part. With encouragement, she is able to rejoin the class and show good motivation, particularly when she has the opportunity to help or demonstrate what she knows.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment: Mila has shown good understanding of the structures and vocabulary worked on this term. She can repeat the target language well and, in many activities, uses the new language with confidence. Her knowledge of the story has improved notably, and she is able to retell parts of it with little support. She also performs very well in activities involving weather, clothing, and colour vocabulary. At times, she still needs help with some specific words and routines, such as taking off and putting on, and she may need further practice to remain consistent when new language is introduced. Continued repetition and reinforcement will help her consolidate her progress even further.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

Comment: Mila is well integrated in the group and can participate positively in class. On the whole, she behaves appropriately and works well with the rest of the students, especially during practical and familiar activities. However, when she becomes frustrated or does not get her way, she may withdraw, refuse to participate, or show signs of anger, which can affect her focus during the lesson. She sometimes needs reminders to stay engaged and remain seated during story time. When she is calm, she responds well and shows a much more cooperative attitude.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 8.00    Good. Mila can understand the question and answers either with a single word or with a structure which is only partially correct.

Final comment

Mila has made good progress this term and has shown that she can understand and use a great deal of the language worked on in class. Her ability to repeat structures, recognise vocabulary, and take part in action activities is very promising. With continued encouragement to manage moments of frustration and to stay engaged throughout the whole lesson, she will be able to keep building confidence and continue developing her English skills successfully.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Participation/Motivation, Learning process, Behaviour) plus Marks and a Final comment, as shown in the examples. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. The notes include moments of frustration and withdrawal — the report should handle these honestly while maintaining an encouraging tone. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections + Marks + Final comment, each with Assessment and Comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - tone: encouraging and positive while honest about challenges - behaviour: frustration and withdrawal handled constructively, not ignored "}]

Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections + Marks + Final comment, each with Assessment and Comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - tone: encouraging and positive while honest about challenges - behaviour: frustration and withdrawal handled constructively, not ignored

prompt.raw:
[{"content":"You are a report card writer for a school teacher. The student notes are the sole source of facts for the report. Every observation, data field, and mark must come from the notes — not from any examples. ## Style & Layout Guide The following are example report cards. Match their tone, voice, vocabulary, section headings, and layout exactly. These examples may differ slightly from the provided additional instructions - in that case, the instructions from the user take precedence. IMPORTANT: Do not copy specific Data field values, Marks, or observations from the examples — only include a Data field or Marks section if that value is present in the student notes. ### Example 1: pdfAvaluacioRo.pdf Kids&Us. Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term --- Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Romane Leclerc Teacher: Thérèse Garnier Level: Oliver 2024/2025 Absences: 1 Frequency of use (audio): Insufficient Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good Comment: Romane displays a keen enthusiasm for learning, particularly evident during vocabulary sessions. She consistently engages with activities, showcasing a willingness to assist in storytelling. Her high level of interest portrays a positive attitude, inspiring her classmates as they work together on different tasks and games. Romane's commitment to participating in exercises, even during challenging activities like singing and miming, reflects her motivation to succeed. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Good Comment: Romane has made commendable progress in her vocabulary acquisition, particularly when discussing Oliver and his family members. She actively engages in activities such as story retelling and dice games, enhancing her understanding of the material. However, Romane has faced challenges with certain grammar concepts, including prepositions. It is essential for her to continue practising these areas to further expand her language skills and confidence in speaking. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Very good Comment: Romane exhibits positive behaviour throughout class, interacting well with her peers and contributing to a collaborative environment. Her involvement in group activities encourages a supportive atmosphere. She shows respect to both classmates and the teacher and expresses encouragement towards others, supporting a healthy learning environment while still developing patience in group settings. Romane's respectful conduct plays an essential role in maintaining the class's focus. Marks Oral Test Mark: 8.00 Good. Romane can understand the question and answers either with a single word or with a structure which is only partially correct. --- Final comment ________________________________________________________________________________ Romane has had a successful year, showing significant growth in her English language skills. Her motivation and engagement in class activities are commendable, and she has gradually acquired vocabulary and grammar structures. Continued practice with audio materials over the summer will undoubtedly support her ongoing learning journey. I look forward to seeing her further develop her skills in the coming year. ### Example 2: pdfAvaluacioCl.pdf Kids&Us. Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term --- Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Clémence Legrand Teacher: Thérèse Garnier Level: Oliver 2024/2025 Absences: 3 Frequency of use (audio): Insufficient Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Satisfactory Comment: Clémence shows an acceptable level of motivation and enthusiasm during class activities, particularly during engaging games like the dice game. Although she encounters challenges with vocabulary, her willingness to participate is commendable. She attempts to interact with lesson materials and her peers despite some struggles. Encouragement to keep engaging positively will help her continue to grow and develop her confidence in classroom interactions. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Poor Comment: Clémence is currently facing challenges with vocabulary, particularly words like young and old, as well as with constructing complete sentences. While she has shown understanding during specific activities, such as identifying clothing for different weather conditions, consistent attention to vocabulary is essential for her progress. To support her learning, it is recommended that Clémence continues to revisit vocabulary at home, helping her to build upon her knowledge and confidence in class. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Good Comment: Clémence is generally a pleasant student but occasionally struggles to focus and stay on task. She sometimes needs reminders to pay attention and limit conversations with her friends during lessons. Despite these distractions, she listens well when prompted. Her behaviour towards group activities is friendly, and she interacts positively with her classmates. By fostering her ability to maintain focus, Clémence can further enhance her learning and classroom experience. Marks Oral Test Mark: 5.90 Needs to improve. Clémence shows a good understanding of the question, but needs to hear the sound of the first word from the teacher to provide an answer and uses only one word when answering. --- Final comment ____________________________________________________________ Clémence has encountered several difficulties this year, particularly with vocabulary retention and attention during class activities. While she displays enthusiasm in group settings, her distractibility hinders her progress. Moving forward, it's crucial that she continues to practise her vocabulary over the summer. With dedicated effort, she has the potential to improve her learning significantly. ### Example 3: pdfAvaluacioLyam.pdf Kids&Us. Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term --- Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Lyam Renard Meli Teacher: Thérèse Garnier Level: Oliver 2024/2025 Absences: 1 Frequency of use (audio): Insufficient Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Excellent Comment: Lyam continues to radiate enthusiasm in class, contributing positively to the learning environment. He eagerly participates in all activities, demonstrating a notable improvement in waiting his turn to speak, even when keen to share. His comfort within the group fosters motivation, making him enjoy lessons more. Lyam approaches every task with readiness and a positive mindset, ensuring his engagement levels remain high and inspiring his peers. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Excellent Comment: Lyam's learning has been exemplary this year. He excels at retelling stories and has shown noticeable improvement in making complete sentences. His ability to describe characteristics of Oliver's family members, indicates his understanding of the material. He quickly identifies and corrects his mistakes, showcasing his awareness and dedication to learning. Overall, his level of engagement is excellent, and he enjoys demonstrating what he has learned during class. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Very good Comment: Lyam exhibits excellent behaviour in the classroom, showing respect for his teachers and kindness towards classmates. He enjoys participating in activities, often assisting others when he can. His eagerness to take part is commendable, though he needs occasional reminders to wait for his turn to speak. Overall, his cooperative approach contributes positively to group dynamics and the classroom environment. Marks Oral Test Mark: 9.90 Excellent. Lyam can understand the question and, in most cases, answers using a structure which is complete and accurate. Excellent work! --- Final comment ____________________________________________ Lyam has had a wonderfully productive year, showcasing outstanding learning and behavioural qualities. He has made significant strides in both his participation and his ability to articulate thoughts in complete sentences. Well done, Lyam, and keep up this exceptional journey in learning. ## Important Additional Instructions from User - Takes precedence over any provided example MOTIVATION AND PARTICIPATION: o Motivation: definition: factor or group of factors that bring about a specific type of behaviour. This section should comment on whether the student shows a positive attitude towards different activities conducted in the classroom: ▪ How does the student come into the classroom? ▪ Do they show willingness to learn? ▪ Are they open to doing new activities? ▪ Are they cooperative with the rest of the group and with the teacher? o Participation: definition: involvement in an event, act or activity. Parents must be informed whether their child participates in the activities that are carried out in the classroom and whether or not they put previously-acquired knowledge, as well as language currently being worked on, into practice. ▪ Outline the degree of participation based on what the student views as being important. For example, he/she is very competitive and always wants to win. He/she gets cross when they don't win. He/she always wants to come first. ▪ Another noteworthy aspect is whether the student holds back when new material is introduced, and whether they take part to a lesser degree than when internalised knowledge is elicited in class. BEHAVIOUR: definition: the way in which a person behaves in a specific or general situation. o Note whether the student is integrated within the group or whether they are easily distracted or unable to focus. o Point out, if necessary, how a student is reacting in light of situations that have had to be tackled with a specific plan. o Explain whether the environment or general mood of the group, or the student's particular mood, affects the student's behaviour. Is the group more easily distracted than normal? Is the student especially tired on rainy days, after public holidays, etc.? 14 LEARNING: definition: the process of studying, practising or experiencing whereby an individual or a group acquires specific knowledge, particularly knowledge required for learning an art form or profession. o Use of structures worked on during the story o Level of learning objectives achieved o Strengths o Weaknesses, together with a plan for improvement and commitment on behalf of the school to work on this aspect. Avoid repetitions in the same section. Keep an encouraging and positive tone ## Student Notes (source of truth — all report content must derive from these) Student: Mila, Class: Oliver Thursday - 2026-05-21: Mila came to class in a very happy mood. She knew some vocabulary and could repeat the structures too. She needed help with the "taking off" and "putting on" words during the weather routines. She got upset because she could not see the Big Book during story time, but did not move to be able to see. She laid down a few times, was asked to sit, and turned her back to me during the whole story because she was angry. She could do the action activity quite well and was happy to participate. - 2026-05-14: The Oliver Thursday students were all great and participated in the activities very well. Mila did close up a bit when she did not get her way, but it got better. They all loved the present activity, all wanted to help hand out the material, and they all got a green face. - 2026-05-14: Mila only listened at first. She was a bit upset because she couldn’t do the stamp and then did not take part in the routines. She then said she had a headache and did take part in the activities. The class could tell who was missing and name the family member, repeat the correct structure each time, pair up the correct sentence with the weather realia item, and say most of the words, although they sometimes needed a bit of help. They needed to move a lot and seemed to get bored by the end of the story. - 2026-05-14: Mila could tell the story with almost no help. She got upset by the end of class because I did not allow her to keep the blu-tack. She refused to take part in the True or False activity and the Dice Game. - 2026-05-14: Mila came today. She knew most of the vocabulary, structures, songs, gestures, and story. They all loved A present for me, Dice Game, and Family Bingo. - 2026-05-14: Mila could combine the mood and the actions very well. She knew the phrase that went with her weather card, and she knew the story quite well. She could already tell part of it and repeat the rest. They learned how to say what they were wearing, and they learned the color white and the color black today. They could do the weather and clothing item combination very well, and they did the worksheet activity very, very well. They also showed that they knew the color vocabulary, which was great. All of them could do the color vocabulary, and I was really proud of them, and their behavior was relatively great today, so I was really happy with them. - 2026-05-14: Mila already knew the structures and understood how to use them, so she could repeat them very well. She could also use the new vocabulary words and understood how to use them during the activities. ## Task Write a report card narrative for this student based on the notes above. Output the report as clean HTML (using

,

Only include structured Data fields (Absences, Marks, Frequency of use, etc.) if those values are explicitly present in the notes. Do not invent or carry over values from the examples. response.output:
```html

Term report (3rd term)

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good

Comment:

  • Mila consistently shows enthusiasm and a positive attitude towards learning, actively engaging in classroom activities.
  • She demonstrates a willingness to participate and apply her knowledge of vocabulary and structures during lessons.
  • Mila’s participation is particularly strong during storytelling and vocabulary exercises, where she contributes confidently.
  • She occasionally closes up when her expectations are not met, such as when she could not see the Big Book or keep the blu-tack, but she regains composure and participates again.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Very good

Comment:

  • Mila has a strong grasp of vocabulary, structures, songs, gestures, and the story content.
  • She effectively combines mood and actions, demonstrating a solid understanding of the material.
  • Mila can retell parts of the story with minimal assistance and uses new vocabulary appropriately during activities.
  • Her ability to pair sentences with weather items and combine clothing items with colors showcases her comprehension and application of learned concepts.
  • She quickly recalls and uses newly introduced vocabulary and structures during activities.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

Comment:

  • Mila generally exhibits positive behavior in the classroom, interacting well with peers and engaging in group activities.
  • She shows respect for the teacher and classmates, contributing to a collaborative environment.
  • Mila has moments of frustration, such as laying down, turning her back during story time, or closing up when upset, but she responds positively to gentle reminders and guidance.
  • Her behavior improves once she regains composure, and she actively participates in subsequent activities.
  • During activities like the Dice Game and Family Bingo, she participates enthusiastically once engaged.

Final comment

________________________________________________________________________________

Mila has made excellent progress this term, demonstrating a strong command of vocabulary and structures. Her enthusiasm and willingness to participate in activities are commendable, even when faced with challenges. While she occasionally experiences moments of frustration, her overall behavior and engagement contribute positively to the classroom environment. Continued encouragement and practice will help her further develop her skills and confidence. Well done, Mila!

```

Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Camille
  • Teacher: [Teacher name not provided in notes]
  • Level: Oliver-Group 2 Saturday

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment:

Camille demonstrates a positive and engaged attitude in class, particularly during activities she enjoys. She shows enthusiasm for interactive tasks such as A Present for Me, the Dice Game, and Family Bingo, where her participation is active and willing. While she occasionally engages in playful distractions with peers like Zoé, she remains attentive overall and responds well to encouragement. Her willingness to repeat vocabulary and phrases when prompted reflects her openness to learning, though she sometimes hesitates when less confident. Camille’s cooperative spirit and enjoyment of group activities contribute positively to the classroom dynamic.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:

Camille has shown solid progress in vocabulary retention, particularly with colors, clothing items, and some prepositions. She understands story elements well, recalling parts of the narrative and demonstrating comprehension in worksheet activities, such as identifying what Oliver and Baby could or couldn’t do. While she requires reminders for correct sentence structures, her memorisation of vocabulary is evident, and she quickly grasps new routines, such as miming weather and actions or describing moods (e.g., “he’s smiling because he’s happy”). The group’s ongoing work on forming questions about physical appearance before guessing identities remains a developing skill for Camille, but her understanding of the material suggests steady improvement with continued practice.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

Comment:

Camille generally behaves well in class, contributing to a friendly and collaborative atmosphere. She interacts playfully with classmates, though at times her joking—such as hiding materials instead of returning them promptly—can briefly disrupt focus. Her attentiveness is notable, especially during storytime and new routines, where she listens eagerly and follows instructions. While she may show occasional hesitation or reduced confidence, she responds positively to encouragement. Camille’s ability to balance enthusiasm with cooperation makes her a pleasant and engaged member of the group.

Final comment

____________________________________________________________

Camille has had a productive term, displaying enthusiasm for interactive activities and a growing command of vocabulary and routines. Her attentive participation in games like A Present for Me and the Dice Game highlights her engagement, while her playful interactions with peers occasionally require gentle redirection. Moving forward, continued practice with sentence structures and questioning techniques will help solidify her progress. With her positive attitude and willingness to learn, Camille is well-positioned to build on her strengths in the coming term.

response.output:
```html

Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Camille
  • Teacher: [Teacher name not provided in notes]
  • Level: Oliver-Group 2 Saturday

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment:

Camille demonstrates a positive and engaged attitude in class, particularly during activities she enjoys. She shows enthusiasm for interactive tasks such as A Present for Me, the Dice Game, and Family Bingo, where her participation is active and willing. While she occasionally engages in playful distractions with peers like Zoé, she remains attentive overall and responds well to encouragement. Her willingness to repeat vocabulary and phrases when prompted reflects her openness to learning, though she sometimes hesitates when less confident. Camille’s cooperative spirit and enjoyment of group activities contribute positively to the classroom dynamic.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:

Camille has shown solid progress in vocabulary retention, particularly with colors, clothing items, and some prepositions. She understands story elements well, recalling parts of the narrative and demonstrating comprehension in worksheet activities, such as identifying what Oliver and Baby could or couldn’t do. While she requires reminders for correct sentence structures, her memorisation of vocabulary is evident, and she quickly grasps new routines, such as miming weather and actions or describing moods (e.g., “he’s smiling because he’s happy”). The group’s ongoing work on forming questions about physical appearance before guessing identities remains a developing skill for Camille, but her understanding of the material suggests steady improvement with continued practice.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

Comment:

Camille generally behaves well in class, contributing to a friendly and collaborative atmosphere. She interacts playfully with classmates, though at times her joking—such as hiding materials instead of returning them promptly—can briefly disrupt focus. Her attentiveness is notable, especially during storytime and new routines, where she listens eagerly and follows instructions. While she may show occasional hesitation or reduced confidence, she responds positively to encouragement. Camille’s ability to balance enthusiasm with cooperation makes her a pleasant and engaged member of the group.

Final comment

____________________________________________________________

Camille has had a productive term, displaying enthusiasm for interactive activities and a growing command of vocabulary and routines. Her attentive participation in games like A Present for Me and the Dice Game highlights her engagement, while her playful interactions with peers occasionally require gentle redirection. Moving forward, continued practice with sentence structures and questioning techniques will help solidify her progress. With her positive attitude and willingness to learn, Camille is well-positioned to build on her strengths in the coming term.

```

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Camille
Teacher: Thérèse Garnier
Level: Oliver 2024/2025

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment:
Camille demonstrates a positive attitude in class, showing enthusiasm for activities such as "A present for me," Family Bingo, and the Dice Game. She enjoys listening to the story and participates actively, though she occasionally engages in playful distractions with peers. Her willingness to repeat after the teacher and her engagement in miming routines indicate a strong motivation to learn. While she sometimes hesitates, encouragement helps her maintain a cheerful and involved demeanour.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:
Camille has shown a solid understanding of vocabulary, particularly colours, clothing items, and some prepositions. She can recite parts of the story and demonstrates memorisation of key terms. However, she, like her peers, still needs reminders for correct sentence structures, especially when forming questions about physical appearance. Her comprehension of new routines, such as weather and mood expressions, is quick, though she is still building confidence in applying these consistently.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

Comment:
Camille is generally attentive and integrates well within the group. While she occasionally plays with materials instead of returning them promptly, her behaviour remains cooperative and light-hearted. She contributes positively to the classroom atmosphere, and her interactions with peers, though sometimes distracting, do not disrupt the learning environment. Her overall conduct reflects a happy and engaged student.

---

Camille has had a productive term, showing enthusiasm for classroom activities and a strong grasp of vocabulary. While she occasionally needs reminders for sentence structures and can be playful with materials, her positive attitude and quick understanding of new routines are commendable. He/she always wants to come first. ▪ Another noteworthy aspect is whether the student holds back when new material is introduced, and whether they take part to a lesser degree than when internalised knowledge is elicited in class. BEHAVIOUR: definition: the way in which a person behaves in a specific or general situation. o Note whether the student is integrated within the group or whether they are easily distracted or unable to focus. o Point out, if necessary, how a student is reacting in light of situations that have had to be tackled with a specific plan. o Explain whether the environment or general mood of the group, or the student's particular mood, affects the student's behaviour. Is the group more easily distracted than normal? Is the student especially tired on rainy days, after public holidays, etc.? 14 LEARNING: definition: the process of studying, practising or experiencing whereby an individual or a group acquires specific knowledge, particularly knowledge required for learning an art form or profession. o Use of structures worked on during the story o Level of learning objectives achieved o Strengths o Weaknesses, together with a plan for improvement and commitment on behalf of the school to work on this aspect. Try not to avoid repetition in the same section Reference report (a known-good output for the same notes — use as a quality benchmark, student name may differ):

Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Camille
  • Teacher: Thérèse Garnier
  • Level: Oliver-Group 2 Saturday 2025/2026
  • Absences: 0
  • Frequency of use (audio): Pass

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good

Comment:
Camille comes into class with a calm and positive attitude, and she shows a pleasant willingness to take part in the different activities proposed. She is generally attentive and open to new tasks, enjoying the story work, the worksheet activities and games such as Present for me, Who’s Missing and the Dice Game. Camille participates well when she feels comfortable, and she shows interest in the language being worked on, especially when she can recall vocabulary and repeat what she has learned. At times she can be a little more hesitant, but with encouragement she responds well and continues to engage positively with the group.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:
Camille has shown a good understanding of the story and the language structures worked on this term. She can remember vocabulary words very well, especially colours, clothing items and some prepositions, and she also demonstrated understanding in activities about what Oliver and Baby could or could not do. She was able to recite parts of the story and to repeat when asked, showing that she is memorising the language and building her confidence. She sometimes needs help to recall the correct structures, particularly when asking questions or using the new language independently, but she shows real understanding and is making steady progress. Continued practice will help her become more secure and more confident when using the structures spontaneously.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Very good

Comment:
Camille has a peaceful, easy-going and attentive manner in class. She is generally well integrated within the group and her calm presence helps create a positive atmosphere. She is usually focused and listens carefully, showing a good level of concentration during activities. On occasion she can be a little playful with the materials or slightly distracted with a classmate, but this does not affect her overall behaviour, which remains very good. Camille responds well to guidance, follows the classroom routine and shows a kind and cooperative attitude towards both the teacher and her peers.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 8.10  Good. Camille can understand the question and answers either with a single word or with a structure which is only partially correct.

Final comment

____________________________________________________________
Camille has had a positive term and has shown good understanding of the story content and vocabulary worked on in class. Her calm and attentive nature, together with her willingness to participate, have contributed well to the group. She is progressing nicely and, with continued practice and confidence, she will keep developing her language skills next term.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Participation/Motivation, Learning process, Behaviour) plus Marks and a Final comment, as shown in the examples. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. The student profile is calm, attentive, and generally positive — the report should maintain a warm and encouraging tone. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections + Marks + Final comment, each with Assessment and Comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - tone: warm, encouraging, matches Oliver-level examples - accuracy: occasional playful behaviour noted without exaggeration "}]

[{"content":"You are a report card writer for a school teacher. The student notes are the sole source of facts for the report. Every observation, data field, and mark must come from the notes — not from any examples. ## Style & Layout Guide The following are example report cards. Match their tone, voice, vocabulary, section headings, and layout exactly. These examples may differ slightly from the provided additional instructions - in that case, the instructions from the user take precedence. IMPORTANT: Do not copy specific Data field values, Marks, or observations from the examples — only include a Data field or Marks section if that value is present in the student notes. ### Example 1: pdfAvaluacioCl.pdf Kids&Us. Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term --- Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Clémence Legrand Teacher: Thérèse Garnier Level: Oliver 2024/2025 Absences: 3 Frequency of use (audio): Insufficient Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Satisfactory Comment: Clémence shows an acceptable level of motivation and enthusiasm during class activities, particularly during engaging games like the dice game. Although she encounters challenges with vocabulary, her willingness to participate is commendable. She attempts to interact with lesson materials and her peers despite some struggles. Encouragement to keep engaging positively will help her continue to grow and develop her confidence in classroom interactions. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Poor Comment: Clémence is currently facing challenges with vocabulary, particularly words like young and old, as well as with constructing complete sentences. While she has shown understanding during specific activities, such as identifying clothing for different weather conditions, consistent attention to vocabulary is essential for her progress. To support her learning, it is recommended that Clémence continues to revisit vocabulary at home, helping her to build upon her knowledge and confidence in class. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Good Comment: Clémence is generally a pleasant student but occasionally struggles to focus and stay on task. She sometimes needs reminders to pay attention and limit conversations with her friends during lessons. Despite these distractions, she listens well when prompted. Her behaviour towards group activities is friendly, and she interacts positively with her classmates. By fostering her ability to maintain focus, Clémence can further enhance her learning and classroom experience. Marks Oral Test Mark: 5.90 Needs to improve. Clémence shows a good understanding of the question, but needs to hear the sound of the first word from the teacher to provide an answer and uses only one word when answering. --- Final comment ____________________________________________________________ Clémence has encountered several difficulties this year, particularly with vocabulary retention and attention during class activities. While she displays enthusiasm in group settings, her distractibility hinders her progress. Moving forward, it's crucial that she continues to practise her vocabulary over the summer. With dedicated effort, she has the potential to improve her learning significantly. ### Example 2: pdfAvaluacioLyam.pdf Kids&Us. Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term --- Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Lyam Renard Meli Teacher: Thérèse Garnier Level: Oliver 2024/2025 Absences: 1 Frequency of use (audio): Insufficient Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Excellent Comment: Lyam continues to radiate enthusiasm in class, contributing positively to the learning environment. He eagerly participates in all activities, demonstrating a notable improvement in waiting his turn to speak, even when keen to share. His comfort within the group fosters motivation, making him enjoy lessons more. Lyam approaches every task with readiness and a positive mindset, ensuring his engagement levels remain high and inspiring his peers. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Excellent Comment: Lyam's learning has been exemplary this year. He excels at retelling stories and has shown noticeable improvement in making complete sentences. His ability to describe characteristics of Oliver's family members, indicates his understanding of the material. He quickly identifies and corrects his mistakes, showcasing his awareness and dedication to learning. Overall, his level of engagement is excellent, and he enjoys demonstrating what he has learned during class. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Very good Comment: Lyam exhibits excellent behaviour in the classroom, showing respect for his teachers and kindness towards classmates. He enjoys participating in activities, often assisting others when he can. His eagerness to take part is commendable, though he needs occasional reminders to wait for his turn to speak. Overall, his cooperative approach contributes positively to group dynamics and the classroom environment. Marks Oral Test Mark: 9.90 Excellent. Lyam can understand the question and, in most cases, answers using a structure which is complete and accurate. Excellent work! --- Final comment ____________________________________________ Lyam has had a wonderfully productive year, showcasing outstanding learning and behavioural qualities. He has made significant strides in both his participation and his ability to articulate thoughts in complete sentences. Well done, Lyam, and keep up this exceptional journey in learning. ### Example 3: Informes 3r trimestre Jules Petit null.pdf Kids&Us. Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term --- Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Jules Petit Teacher: Thérèse Garnier Level: Oliver 2024/2025 Absences: 0 Frequency of use (audio): Pass Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good Comment: Jules exhibits a generally cheerful and positive disposition in class. Even though he can become distracted, he shows enthusiasm during activities such as the Dice game. Jules participates when called upon, particularly enjoying activities like 'Who is Missing', though he requires encouragement to engage in storytelling. His willingness to repeat after the teacher indicates a desire to participate and learn, reflecting his potential for increased motivation in class. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Good Comment: Jules has encountered challenges with certain concepts, particularly with vocabulary related to clothing and sentence structure. He has struggled with making complete sentences and understanding prepositions, showing a need for continued practice in these areas. Despite these hurdles, Jules has shown some progress with vocabulary comprehension. Encouragement and regular reinforcement will be key to helping Jules develop his language skills further and build his confidence in these areas. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Good Comment: Jules typically displays kind behaviour towards both his classmates and teachers. He enjoys sharing materials and playing collaborative games, showing a positive attitude when participating. However, he sometimes becomes distracted and has a tendency to zone out, which can affect his focus. At times, he needs gentle reminders to refocus his attention, especially during more challenging tasks where he tends to retreat into his own world. Marks Oral Test Mark: 7.60 Good. Jules can understand the question and answers either with a single word or with a structure which is only partially correct. --- Final comment ____________________________________________________________ This year, Jules has shown moments of happiness and kindness, contributing positively to the classroom environment. While he faces difficulties in maintaining focus and constructing complete sentences, he has participated well in various activities. Looking ahead, consistent practice, especially through audio resources during the summer, can help him realise his full potential and boost his confidence in learning next year. ## Important Additional Instructions from User - Takes precedence over any provided example 5.2. KIDS: MOTIVATION AND PARTICIPATION: o Motivation: definition: factor or group of factors that bring about a specific type of behaviour. This section should comment on whether the student shows a positive attitude towards different activities conducted in the classroom: ▪ How does the student come into the classroom? ▪ Do they show willingness to learn? ▪ Are they open to doing new activities? ▪ Are they cooperative with the rest of the group and with the teacher? o Participation: definition: involvement in an event, act or activity. Parents must be informed whether their child participates in the activities that are carried out in the classroom and whether or not they put previously-acquired knowledge, as well as language currently being worked on, into practice. ▪ Outline the degree of participation based on what the student views as being important. For example, he/she is very competitive and always wants to win. He/she gets cross when they don't win. He/she always wants to come first. ▪ Another noteworthy aspect is whether the student holds back when new material is introduced, and whether they take part to a lesser degree than when internalised knowledge is elicited in class. BEHAVIOUR: definition: the way in which a person behaves in a specific or general situation. o Note whether the student is integrated within the group or whether they are easily distracted or unable to focus. o Point out, if necessary, how a student is reacting in light of situations that have had to be tackled with a specific plan. o Explain whether the environment or general mood of the group, or the student's particular mood, affects the student's behaviour. Is the group more easily distracted than normal? Is the student especially tired on rainy days, after public holidays, etc.? 14 LEARNING: definition: the process of studying, practising or experiencing whereby an individual or a group acquires specific knowledge, particularly knowledge required for learning an art form or profession. o Use of structures worked on during the story o Level of learning objectives achieved o Strengths o Weaknesses, together with a plan for improvement and commitment on behalf of the school to work on this aspect. Try not to avoid repetition in the same section ## Student Notes (source of truth — all report content must derive from these) Student: Camille, Class: Oliver-Group 2 Saturday - 2026-05-14: Camille was involved in Zoé being playful with the material, and she was attentive overall. She knew parts of the story, understood the worksheet activity, and loved the Present for me activity. - 2026-05-14: Camille could recite the story partially. They all got bored by the end of it. They all could do the worksheet activity about what Oliver and Baby could or couldn’t do very well. They needed my help to remind the correct structures for a lot of the activities, but they did show a real understanding and a certain memorisation of the vocabulary. They loved the Who’s missing activity. - 2026-05-14: Camille was a bit distracting with Zoé, but it was alright. The group also did very well on the worksheet activity, loved a present for me and the Dice Game, and they are still learning to ask questions about the person's physical appearance before just asking who it is. It is a work in progress. - 2026-05-14: Camille was not always giving the material back directly when asked, and was hiding it jokingly. The group loved the activities, including "A present for me," Family Bingo, and Dice Game. - 2026-05-14: Camille was very attentive. She could remember the vocabulary words very well, tell the colors and the clothing items very well, and could remember some prepositions. She was very happy to listen to the story and seemed to really enjoy the new story. - 2026-05-14: Camille seemed to really enjoy the new story. She could repeat when I asked her to repeat, quickly understood the new weather and action miming routine, and quickly understood the new mood routine with 'he's smiling because he's happy.' She was a bit less confident today and seemed more hesitant, but I kept encouraging her and I think she felt quite happy. I was really proud of their behaviour. ## Task Write a report card narrative for this student based on the notes above. Output the report as clean HTML (using

,

Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Camille
Teacher: Thérèse Garnier
Level: Oliver 2024/2025

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment:
Camille demonstrates a positive attitude in class, showing enthusiasm for activities such as "A present for me," Family Bingo, and the Dice Game. She enjoys listening to the story and participates actively, though she occasionally engages in playful distractions with peers. Her willingness to repeat after the teacher and her engagement in miming routines indicate a strong motivation to learn. While she sometimes hesitates, encouragement helps her maintain a cheerful and involved demeanour.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:
Camille has shown a solid understanding of vocabulary, particularly colours, clothing items, and some prepositions. She can recite parts of the story and demonstrates memorisation of key terms. However, she, like her peers, still needs reminders for correct sentence structures, especially when forming questions about physical appearance. Her comprehension of new routines, such as weather and mood expressions, is quick, though she is still building confidence in applying these consistently.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

Comment:
Camille is generally attentive and integrates well within the group. While she occasionally plays with materials instead of returning them promptly, her behaviour remains cooperative and light-hearted. She contributes positively to the classroom atmosphere, and her interactions with peers, though sometimes distracting, do not disrupt the learning environment. Her overall conduct reflects a happy and engaged student.

---

Final comment

____________________________________________________________

Camille has had a productive term, showing enthusiasm for classroom activities and a strong grasp of vocabulary. While she occasionally needs reminders for sentence structures and can be playful with materials, her positive attitude and quick understanding of new routines are commendable. With continued practice, particularly in forming questions and maintaining focus, she will further develop her confidence and skills.

She sometimes needs help to recall the correct structures, particularly when asking questions or using the new language independently, but she shows real understanding and is making steady progress. Continued practice will help her become more secure and more confident when using the structures spontaneously.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Very good

Comment:
Camille has a peaceful, easy-going and attentive manner in class. She is generally well integrated within the group and her calm presence helps create a positive atmosphere. She is usually focused and listens carefully, showing a good level of concentration during activities. On occasion she can be a little playful with the materials or slightly distracted with a classmate, but this does not affect her overall behaviour, which remains very good. Camille responds well to guidance, follows the classroom routine and shows a kind and cooperative attitude towards both the teacher and her peers.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 8.10  Good. Camille can understand the question and answers either with a single word or with a structure which is only partially correct.

Final comment

____________________________________________________________
Camille has had a positive term and has shown good understanding of the story content and vocabulary worked on in class. Her calm and attentive nature, together with her willingness to participate, have contributed well to the group. She is progressing nicely and, with continued practice and confidence, she will keep developing her language skills next term.

Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Camille
  • Teacher: Thérèse Garnier
  • Level: Oliver-Group 2 Saturday 2025/2026
  • Absences: 0
  • Frequency of use (audio): Pass

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good

Comment:
Camille comes into class with a calm and positive attitude, and she shows a pleasant willingness to take part in the different activities proposed. She is generally attentive and open to new tasks, enjoying the story work, the worksheet activities and games such as Present for me, Who’s Missing and the Dice Game. Camille participates well when she feels comfortable, and she shows interest in the language being worked on, especially when she can recall vocabulary and repeat what she has learned. At times she can be a little more hesitant, but with encouragement she responds well and continues to engage positively with the group.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:
Camille has shown a good understanding of the story and the language structures worked on this term. She can remember vocabulary words very well, especially colours, clothing items and some prepositions, and she also demonstrated understanding in activities about what Oliver and Baby could or could not do. She was able to recite parts of the story and to repeat when asked, showing that she is memorising the language and building her confidence. She sometimes needs help to recall the correct structures, particularly when asking questions or using the new language independently, but she shows real understanding and is making steady progress. Continued practice will help her become more secure and more confident when using the structures spontaneously.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Very good

Comment:
Camille has a peaceful, easy-going and attentive manner in class. She is generally well integrated within the group and her calm presence helps create a positive atmosphere. She is usually focused and listens carefully, showing a good level of concentration during activities. On occasion she can be a little playful with the materials or slightly distracted with a classmate, but this does not affect her overall behaviour, which remains very good. Camille responds well to guidance, follows the classroom routine and shows a kind and cooperative attitude towards both the teacher and her peers.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 8.10  Good. Camille can understand the question and answers either with a single word or with a structure which is only partially correct.

Final comment

____________________________________________________________
Camille has had a positive term and has shown good understanding of the story content and vocabulary worked on in class. Her calm and attentive nature, together with her willingness to participate, have contributed well to the group. She is progressing nicely and, with continued practice and confidence, she will keep developing her language skills next term.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Participation/Motivation, Learning process, Behaviour) plus Marks and a Final comment, as shown in the examples. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. The student profile is calm, attentive, and generally positive — the report should maintain a warm and encouraging tone. response.output:
Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Camille
Teacher: [Teacher Name]
Level: Oliver 2024/2025

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment:

Camille demonstrates a positive attitude towards classroom activities, showing enthusiasm for storytelling and interactive games. She participates actively in group tasks such as the worksheet activity, "Who’s missing," and "A present for me," displaying a willingness to engage with both new and familiar material. While she occasionally interacts playfully with peers, her overall cooperation and involvement contribute positively to the learning environment. Her attentiveness during storytelling and miming routines indicates a readiness to participate, though moments of hesitation suggest room for further confidence-building.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:

Camille has shown a solid understanding of vocabulary, particularly colors, clothing items, and prepositions, as well as the ability to recall parts of the story. She successfully completes worksheet activities with guidance, demonstrating comprehension of structures like what Oliver and Baby could or couldn’t do. Her participation in miming routines and mood-based activities ("he’s smiling because he’s happy") reflects a quick grasp of new concepts. While she occasionally requires reminders to reinforce correct structures, her memorisation and engagement indicate steady progress. Continued practice with storytelling and interactive exercises will further solidify her learning.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

Comment:

Camille generally integrates well within the group, contributing to a positive classroom atmosphere. She enjoys collaborative activities like "Family Bingo" and "Dice Game," and her behaviour during storytelling is attentive and enthusiastic. While she occasionally engages in playful distractions with peers, her overall responsiveness to instructions and willingness to participate remain strong. Her mood and engagement appear to fluctuate slightly, with moments of hesitation, but encouragement helps her regain confidence. The group’s positive energy further supports her participation.

Final comment

____________________________________________________________

Camille has shown consistent enthusiasm and attentiveness in class, particularly in activities involving storytelling, vocabulary recall, and interactive games. Her ability to remember and apply new concepts, such as prepositions and clothing items, highlights her growing confidence. While she occasionally needs gentle reminders to stay focused or return materials, her overall behaviour and participation reflect a positive learning attitude. With continued encouragement and reinforcement of structures, she is well-positioned to build on her progress and enjoy further success in her language development.

You respond with a JSON object with this structure: {reason: string, pass: boolean, score: number} Examples: Hello world Content contains a greeting {"reason": "the content contains the word 'Hello'", "pass": true, "score": 1.0} Avast ye swabs, repel the invaders! Does not speak like a pirate {"reason": "'avast ye' is a common pirate term", "pass": false, "score": 0.0}"},{"role":"user","content":"

Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Camille
  • Level: Oliver-Group 2 Saturday

Participation/Motivation

Comment:

Camille was attentive overall and showed a positive attitude towards the activities in class. She loved the Present for me activity and seemed to really enjoy the new story. She also enjoyed the Who’s missing activity, Family Bingo and the Dice Game, and she was very happy to listen to the story. Camille could repeat when asked, and she quickly understood the new weather and action miming routine as well as the new mood routine with “he’s smiling because he’s happy.” She was a bit less confident and more hesitant at times, but she remained engaged and receptive to encouragement.

Learning process

Comment:

Camille knew parts of the story and could recite it partially. She understood the worksheet activity, and she did very well on the worksheet about what Oliver and Baby could or couldn’t do. She needed help to remember the correct structures for many of the activities, but she showed a real understanding and a certain memorisation of the vocabulary. She could remember the vocabulary words very well, including colours and clothing items, and she could also remember some prepositions. She is still learning to ask questions about the person’s physical appearance before just asking who it is, and this remains a work in progress.

Behaviour

Comment:

Camille was very attentive, although she was a bit distracting with Zoé at times. She was not always giving the material back directly when asked and was hiding it jokingly. Even so, her behaviour was very positive, and I was really proud of it.

Final comment

____________________________________________________________

Camille has shown good attention in class and has enjoyed the new story and the different activities. She remembers vocabulary very well, especially colours, clothing items and some prepositions, and she is beginning to use the correct structures with support. She is still working on asking questions about a person’s physical appearance, and with continued encouragement she can keep building her confidence.
