KIDS: MOTIVATION AND PARTICIPATION: o Motivation: definition: factor or group of factors that bring about a specific type of behaviour. This section should comment on whether the student shows a positive attitude towards different activities conducted in the classroom: ▪ How does the student come into the classroom? ▪ Do they show willingness to learn? ▪ Are they open to doing new activities? ▪ Are they cooperative with the rest of the group and with the teacher? o Participation: definition: involvement in an event, act or activity. Parents must be informed whether their child participates in the activities that are carried out in the classroom and whether or not they put previously-acquired knowledge, as well as language currently being worked on, into practice. ▪ Outline the degree of participation based on what the student views as being important. For example, he/she is very competitive and always wants to win. He/she gets cross when they don't win. He/she always wants to come first. ▪ Another noteworthy aspect is whether the student holds back when new material is introduced, and whether they take part to a lesser degree than when internalised knowledge is elicited in class. BEHAVIOUR: definition: the way in which a person behaves in a specific or general situation. o Note whether the student is integrated within the group or whether they are easily distracted or unable to focus. o Point out, if necessary, how a student is reacting in light of situations that have had to be tackled with a specific plan. o Explain whether the environment or general mood of the group, or the student's particular mood, affects the student's behaviour. Is the group more easily distracted than normal? Is the student especially tired on rainy days, after public holidays, etc.? 14 LEARNING: definition: the process of studying, practising or experiencing whereby an individual or a group acquires specific knowledge, particularly knowledge required for learning an art form or profession. o Use of structures worked on during the story o Level of learning objectives achieved o Strengths o Weaknesses, together with a plan for improvement and commitment on behalf of the school to work on this aspect. Try not to avoid repetition in the same section Reference report (a known-good output for the same notes — use as a quality benchmark, student name may differ):

Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Camille
  • Teacher: Thérèse Garnier
  • Level: Oliver-Group 2 Saturday 2025/2026
  • Absences: 0
  • Frequency of use (audio): Pass

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good

Comment:
Camille comes into class with a calm and positive attitude, and she shows a pleasant willingness to take part in the different activities proposed. She is generally attentive and open to new tasks, enjoying the story work, the worksheet activities and games such as Present for me, Who’s Missing and the Dice Game. Camille participates well when she feels comfortable, and she shows interest in the language being worked on, especially when she can recall vocabulary and repeat what she has learned. At times she can be a little more hesitant, but with encouragement she responds well and continues to engage positively with the group.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:
Camille has shown a good understanding of the story and the language structures worked on this term. She can remember vocabulary words very well, especially colours, clothing items and some prepositions, and she also demonstrated understanding in activities about what Oliver and Baby could or could not do. She was able to recite parts of the story and to repeat when asked, showing that she is memorising the language and building her confidence. She sometimes needs help to recall the correct structures, particularly when asking questions or using the new language independently, but she shows real understanding and is making steady progress. Continued practice will help her become more secure and more confident when using the structures spontaneously.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Very good

Comment:
Camille has a peaceful, easy-going and attentive manner in class. She is generally well integrated within the group and her calm presence helps create a positive atmosphere. She is usually focused and listens carefully, showing a good level of concentration during activities. On occasion she can be a little playful with the materials or slightly distracted with a classmate, but this does not affect her overall behaviour, which remains very good. Camille responds well to guidance, follows the classroom routine and shows a kind and cooperative attitude towards both the teacher and her peers.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 8.10  Good. Camille can understand the question and answers either with a single word or with a structure which is only partially correct.

Final comment

____________________________________________________________
Camille has had a positive term and has shown good understanding of the story content and vocabulary worked on in class. Her calm and attentive nature, together with her willingness to participate, have contributed well to the group. She is progressing nicely and, with continued practice and confidence, she will keep developing her language skills next term.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Participation/Motivation, Learning process, Behaviour) plus Marks and a Final comment, as shown in the examples. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. The student profile is calm, attentive, and generally positive — the report should maintain a warm and encouraging tone. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections + Marks + Final comment, each with Assessment and Comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - tone: warm, encouraging, matches Oliver-level examples - accuracy: occasional playful behaviour noted without exaggeration "}]

response.output:
Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Camille
  • Level: Oliver-Group 2 Saturday

Participation/Motivation

Comment:

Camille was attentive overall and showed a positive attitude towards the activities in class. She loved the Present for me activity and seemed to really enjoy the new story. She also enjoyed the Who’s missing activity, Family Bingo and the Dice Game, and she was very happy to listen to the story. Camille could repeat when asked, and she quickly understood the new weather and action miming routine as well as the new mood routine with “he’s smiling because he’s happy.” She was a bit less confident and more hesitant at times, but she remained engaged and receptive to encouragement.

Learning process

Comment:

Camille knew parts of the story and could recite it partially. She understood the worksheet activity, and she did very well on the worksheet about what Oliver and Baby could or couldn’t do. She needed help to remember the correct structures for many of the activities, but she showed a real understanding and a certain memorisation of the vocabulary. She could remember the vocabulary words very well, including colours and clothing items, and she could also remember some prepositions. She is still learning to ask questions about the person’s physical appearance before just asking who it is, and this remains a work in progress.

Behaviour

Comment:

Camille was very attentive, although she was a bit distracting with Zoé at times. She was not always giving the material back directly when asked and was hiding it jokingly. Even so, her behaviour was very positive, and I was really proud of it.

Final comment

____________________________________________________________

Camille has shown good attention in class and has enjoyed the new story and the different activities. She remembers vocabulary very well, especially colours, clothing items and some prepositions, and she is beginning to use the correct structures with support. She is still working on asking questions about a person’s physical appearance, and with continued encouragement she can keep building her confidence.

Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Camille
  • Teacher: Thérèse Garnier
  • Level: Oliver-Group 2 Saturday 2025/2026
  • Absences: 0
  • Frequency of use (audio): Pass

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good

Comment:
Camille comes into class with a calm and positive attitude, and she shows a pleasant willingness to take part in the different activities proposed. She is generally attentive and open to new tasks, enjoying the story work, the worksheet activities and games such as Present for me, Who’s Missing and the Dice Game. Camille participates well when she feels comfortable, and she shows interest in the language being worked on, especially when she can recall vocabulary and repeat what she has learned. At times she can be a little more hesitant, but with encouragement she responds well and continues to engage positively with the group.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:
Camille has shown a good understanding of the story and the language structures worked on this term. She can remember vocabulary words very well, especially colours, clothing items and some prepositions, and she also demonstrated understanding in activities about what Oliver and Baby could or could not do. She was able to recite parts of the story and to repeat when asked, showing that she is memorising the language and building her confidence. She sometimes needs help to recall the correct structures, particularly when asking questions or using the new language independently, but she shows real understanding and is making steady progress. Continued practice will help her become more secure and more confident when using the structures spontaneously.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Very good

Comment:
Camille has a peaceful, easy-going and attentive manner in class. She is generally well integrated within the group and her calm presence helps create a positive atmosphere. She is usually focused and listens carefully, showing a good level of concentration during activities. On occasion she can be a little playful with the materials or slightly distracted with a classmate, but this does not affect her overall behaviour, which remains very good. Camille responds well to guidance, follows the classroom routine and shows a kind and cooperative attitude towards both the teacher and her peers.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 8.10  Good. Camille can understand the question and answers either with a single word or with a structure which is only partially correct.

Final comment

____________________________________________________________
Camille has had a positive term and has shown good understanding of the story content and vocabulary worked on in class. Her calm and attentive nature, together with her willingness to participate, have contributed well to the group. She is progressing nicely and, with continued practice and confidence, she will keep developing her language skills next term.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Participation/Motivation, Learning process, Behaviour) plus Marks and a Final comment, as shown in the examples. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. The student profile is calm, attentive, and generally positive — the report should maintain a warm and encouraging tone. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections + Marks + Final comment, each with Assessment and Comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - tone: warm, encouraging, matches Oliver-level examples - accuracy: occasional playful behaviour noted without exaggeration

Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term

—

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Camille
Level: Oliver-Group 2 Saturday

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good
Comment:
Camille shows enthusiasm during classroom activities, particularly enjoying games like 'Present for me', 'Who's missing', and the Dice Game. She participates in group activities and demonstrates a willingness to engage with lesson materials. While she sometimes needs reminders about classroom routines, her overall participation is positive and she contributes to the learning environment.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good
Comment:
Camille demonstrates a good understanding of vocabulary, colors, clothing items, and prepositions. She can recall parts of stories and shows memorization of new vocabulary. While she occasionally needs support with sentence structures, she quickly grasps new routines and concepts. Her ability to participate in activities indicates progress in her learning process.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good
Comment:
Camille generally exhibits good behavior in class. She is attentive during activities and enjoys participating in group games. While she can be distracting at times (particularly with Zoé) and occasionally needs reminders about classroom procedures, her overall conduct is positive. The teacher noted being proud of her behavior on several occasions.

Final comment

____________________________________________________________

Camille has shown enthusiasm and engagement in classroom activities this term. She demonstrates good understanding of vocabulary and concepts, though she occasionally needs support with structures. Her participation in group activities is a strength, and her behavior is generally positive. With continued practice and encouragement, she can build on her current progress.

You respond with a JSON object with this structure: {reason: string, pass: boolean, score: number} Examples: Hello world Content contains a greeting {"reason": "the content contains the word 'Hello'", "pass": true, "score": 1.0} Avast ye swabs, repel the invaders! Does not speak like a pirate {"reason": "'avast ye' is a common pirate term", "pass": false, "score": 0.0}"},{"role":"user","content":"

Kids&Us.

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Hugo
Teacher: Hélène Marchal
Level: Fairy Tales 2 2025/2026

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent
Comment: Hugo is a motivated and attentive student who consistently participates with enthusiasm. He is polite, cooperative, and maintains a positive attitude in class. He is comfortable being corrected and actively engages in discussions and activities. His willingness to help classmates, such as assisting Nina with spelling, contributes to a supportive learning environment.

Work

Assessment (Work): Excellent
Comment: Hugo approaches his work seriously and demonstrates strong engagement in all activities. He completes exercises well, participates actively in games, and shows a good ability to understand and apply new vocabulary and structures. His dedication to correcting mistakes and improving his work is commendable.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Very good
Comment: Hugo performs well in assessments and class activities. He demonstrates a solid understanding of grammatical structures and can use the target language effectively in both oral and written tasks. While he occasionally makes mistakes with pronunciation, verb tense (e.g., "didn’t found"), articles, and third-person singular forms, he is quick to self-correct when errors are pointed out. His ability to summarize chapters, form questions, and engage in discussions reflects his strong grasp of the material. However, he sometimes struggles to independently use new vocabulary in meaningful sentences.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: Not applicable.

Writing Mark: Not applicable.

Homework mark: Not applicable.

---

Final comment

Hugo has been a highly motivated and engaged student throughout the term. His positive attitude, willingness to correct mistakes, and active participation have made him a pleasure to teach. To continue progressing, Hugo should focus on reinforcing grammatical structures, particularly third-person singular forms and articles, and practice using new vocabulary in context. His enthusiasm and cooperative spirit ensure he is well-prepared for future success.

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Hugo
  • Teacher: Hélène Marchal
  • Level: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday
  • Absences: 0

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent

Comment:
Hugo has continued to show a very positive attitude in class. He is polite, attentive, and motivated, and he always takes part in the activities enthusiastically. He is comfortable being corrected and remains willing to try again until he finds the right answer. His friendly and cooperative nature makes him a pleasure to teach and contributes positively to the class atmosphere.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good

Comment:
Hugo works well in class and completes the activities seriously and to the best of his ability. He participates actively in games, listening tasks, reading, and oral exercises, and he usually follows the teacher’s instructions carefully. He is able to correct his mistakes when they are pointed out to him, which shows good engagement with his learning. He should continue to focus on pronunciation, spelling, and the use of grammar structures such as articles, connectors, and third person singular forms to strengthen his accuracy.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

Comment:
Hugo has demonstrated a solid understanding of the vocabulary and structures covered this term. He can express himself quite well orally, answer questions using the target language, and read texts with good comprehension. He also performs well in listening activities and is able to identify and correct many of his own mistakes. However, he still sometimes makes pronunciation errors and occasional grammar mistakes, especially with verb endings, articles, and tense forms. With continued practice, he will become more accurate and fluent.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 7.80    Good.

Hugo speaks with confidence and can communicate his ideas clearly in class. He is usually able to use the target language well and answers questions appropriately. He should continue practising pronunciation and pay attention to third person singular endings and article use to improve his accuracy further.

Writing Mark: 7.20    Good.

Hugo shows a good ability to complete written tasks and understands the vocabulary and grammar worked on in class. He can correct most spelling mistakes when they are pointed out, but he should keep working on careful spelling and on applying grammar rules more consistently, especially in longer written exercises.

Hugo completes his homework well and shows a responsible attitude towards his learning. Regular homework has supported his progress this term. Continuing to review vocabulary, pronunciation, and grammar at home will help him consolidate what he has learned and make even greater progress next term.

---

Final comment

Hugo has had a very positive term. He is motivated, respectful, and engaged in class, and he always shows a good attitude towards learning. His oral participation, reading, and listening skills are strong, and he is increasingly able to correct himself when he makes mistakes. Over the summer, he should continue to practise pronunciation, revise grammar structures, and read aloud regularly to improve his fluency and accuracy for the next academic year.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Behaviour, Work, Performance) plus a Final comment, as shown in the reference. This tests whether a second student in the same class produces a comparable-quality report. The student is strong across all dimensions with consistent motivation and self-correction habits. IMPORTANT: Do not invent Data fields (Marks, Homework marks, Absences, Audio frequency, or other numerical metadata) — only include facts traceable to the source notes. Data

Name:            Hugo Dubois

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour):    Excellent

asks questions when unsure. His enthusiasm and polite

Comment:

speaking and writing. His positive attitude and respectful nature benefit the classroom environment. To continue his
progress over the summer, he should focus on regular reading, writing practice, and speaking activities to reinforce
vocabulary and grammar, helping to build fluency and accuracy for the next academic year."

plus a Final comment, as shown in the reference. This tests whether a
second student in the same class produces a comparable-quality report. The student
is strong across all dimensions with consistent motivation and self-correction
habits. IMPORTANT: Do not invent Data fields (Marks, Homework marks, Absences,
Audio frequency, or other numerical metadata) — only include facts traceable to
the source notes. Score 1-5 on:
- structure: three sections (Behaviour/Work/Performance) + Final comment
- grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations, no
  fabricated homework or test marks)
- tone: constructive and encouraging, matches Fairy Tales 2 examples
- consistency: quality comparable to reference; specific weaknesses (pronunciation,
  spelling, grammar) acknowledged accurately

The student notes are the sole source of facts for the report.
Every observation, data field, and mark must come from the notes — not from any examples.

## Style & Layout Guide
The following are example report cards. Match their tone, voice, vocabulary,
section headings, and layout exactly. These examples may differ slightly from the provided
additional instructions - in that case, the instructions from the user take precedence.
IMPORTANT: Do not copy specific Data field values, Marks, or observations from the examples —
only include a Data field or Marks section if that value is present in the student notes.

## Student Notes (source of truth — all report content must derive from these)
Student: Hugo, Class: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday

- 2026-05-14: The Fairy Tales 2 students were all motivated. They could all do the exercises well enough, make sentences with a comparative, understand and hear the mistakes in the songs, and read very well. Hugo was sometimes making a couple of mistakes with the pronunciation. He could complete the gaps during one of the listening exercises.
- 2026-05-14: The group went well overall: they were all polite, motivated, and interested. They could remember parts of the story quite well, answer using full structures, understand the vocabulary well, and do the games well too. Hugo struggled a bit with pronunciation, but could repeat the correct words. In the My Talent Body 1 part, he made spelling mistakes like “wich” instead of “which”.
- 2026-05-14: Hugo was happy to be there and took part in the activities enthusiastically. He loved the Dice Game, drawing and explaining expressions. He still made a few mistakes orally, but he is always willing to correct himself. He could tell me riddles, did his exercises seriously, and reviewed the different articles in texts. He was fun to teach thanks to his motivation. He is always comfortable with being corrected and finding the correct answers.
- 2026-05-14: Hugo was attentive and motivated as always. Hugo and Nina had forgotten how to use connectors such as although and despite. Hugo still makes some pronunciation mistakes and sometimes makes tense mistakes like “didn’t found.” The class could do the exercises quite well and understand the new vocabulary very well too, and they all did an effort to express themselves.
- 2026-05-14: They could all express themselves rather well when talking about food groups, dishes and meals. They could use the structures given by the teacher very well, and they worked on an argumentative text quite well. Hugo made a couple of grammatical mistakes, like using a verb in -ing after could, and he did some spelling mistakes, but he could correct them almost all directly when they were pointed out to him. Hugo and Clara could talk about obligations and prohibitions very well when talking about primary and secondary school rules. Hugo sometimes needs a bit of help with pronunciation. Nina and Hugo needed a bit of help for inspiration, and Hugo asked me the purpose of the connector before using it.
- 2026-05-14: Hugo was calm and attentive. He did the work he was asked and did it as well as he could. He could read the chapter quite well, without pronunciation mistakes, but he was not totally fluent. He could summarize the chapters using the target language written on the board rather well, make questions from the target language, answer using it too, and understand a song and answer questions about it. He could form a question and an answer using “would rather…than…” and “prefer… to…”. He could not really use vocabulary words and make sentences with them that showed understanding of the words.
- 2026-05-14: Hugo could answer questions using the target language quite well. They could do the listening activity quite well and could report what the characters had said quite well, although I needed to tell Hugo to mind the ‘s’ at the 3rd person singular. Clara and Hugo struggled a bit with the grammar revision, but still enjoyed the game. Their game revision of the vocabulary was better. Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Hugo
Teacher: Hélène Marchal
Level: Fairy Tales 2 2025/2026

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent
Comment: Hugo is a motivated and attentive student who consistently participates with enthusiasm. He is polite, cooperative, and maintains a positive attitude in class. He is comfortable being corrected and actively engages in discussions and activities. His willingness to help classmates, such as assisting Nina with spelling, contributes to a supportive learning environment.

Work

Assessment (Work): Excellent
Comment: Hugo approaches his work seriously and demonstrates strong engagement in all activities. He completes exercises well, participates actively in games, and shows a good ability to understand and apply new vocabulary and structures. Performance

Assessment (Performance): Very good
Comment: Hugo performs well in assessments and class activities. He demonstrates a solid understanding of grammatical structures and can use the target language effectively in both oral and written tasks. While he occasionally makes mistakes with pronunciation, verb tense (e.g., "didn’t found"), articles, and third-person singular forms, he is quick to self-correct when errors are pointed out. Marks

Oral Test Mark: Not applicable.

While he occasionally struggles with pronunciation, verb conjugations, and articles, his ability to self-correct and apply feedback demonstrates his commitment to improvement. To continue progressing, Hugo should focus on reinforcing grammatical structures, particularly third-person singular forms and articles, and practice using new vocabulary in context. prompt.raw:
[{"content":"You are a report card writer for a school teacher.
The student notes are the sole source of facts for the report.
Every observation, data field, and mark must come from the notes — not from any examples.

## Style & Layout Guide
The following are example report cards. Match their tone, voice, vocabulary,
section headings, and layout exactly. These examples may differ slightly from the provided
additional instructions - in that case, the instructions from the user take precedence.

### Example 1: Informes 3r trimestre Clara Petit  Excellent.pdf
Kids&Us.

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 1, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name:                Clara Petit
Teacher:             Hélène Marchal
Level:               Tweens 3 2024/2025
Absences:            0

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour):        Excellent
Comment:                        Clara continues to be a kind, responsible, and respectful
                                student. She is always polite and considerate towards both
                                her classmates and teachers. She actively listens, engages in
                                class discussions, and consistently asks for clarification
                                when she doesn't understand something. Her positive and
                                cooperative nature contributes to a welcoming and
                                supportive classroom environment.

Work

Assessment (Work):             Excellent
Comment:                        Clara remains an engaged and hardworking student. She
                                participates actively in class and completes her tasks with
                                enthusiasm. She consistently takes initiative in her learning
                                and demonstrates great responsibility. However, she should
                                make an effort to revise more regularly and complete all
                                assigned homework to reinforce her understanding of the
                                material. With improved homework habits, she can continue
                                to strengthen her learning outcomes.

Performance

Assessment (Performance):       Excellent
Comment:                        Clara continues to excel in her academic performance. She
                                has achieved excellent results in her assessments and
                                demonstrates a strong grasp of vocabulary, grammar, and
                                comprehension. Her test results are consistently impressive,
                                and she shows great confidence in both oral and written
                                tasks. She can extract key details from texts and express her
                                thoughts clearly and coherently. Clara's ability to analyse and
                                interpret information demonstrates her deep understanding
                                of the subject matter.

Marks

Oral Test Mark:                10.00     Excellent.

                                Clara performed exceptionally well in her oral test. She
                                communicates with confidence and demonstrates a solid
                                understanding of the language structures covered in class. Her ability
                                to articulate ideas fluently and accurately is commendable. With
                                continued practice and exposure to varied speaking opportunities,
                                she will further enhance her fluency and expressiveness.

Writing Mark:                  9.80      Excellent.

                                Clara's written work continues to be of high quality. She constructs
                                well-structured and coherent texts with minimal grammatical errors.
                                With continued focus and revision before tests, she has the potential
                                to achieve even greater results. Regular proofreading and expanding
                                her vocabulary will also support her ongoing improvement.

---

Homework mark:    0.00    Needs to improve.

Clara did not complete all of her assigned homework this term,
explaining that she was busy with school exams. While her in-class
performance remains excellent, she is encouraged to develop a more
consistent study routine that balances her school commitments.
Regular revision and completion of homework will help reinforce her
learning and sustain her strong academic progress.

---

Final comment

Clara has proven to be a bright, motivated, and dedicated student. She consistently demonstrates a strong command
of English and maintains excellent behaviour and participation in class. Her positive attitude, strong academic skills,
and willingness to engage in discussions have contributed greatly to her success. With improved consistency in
completing homework and regular revision, she is well-positioned to continue excelling. Over the summer, Clara can
prepare for next year by reading a variety of English texts, practising writing regularly, and reviewing key grammar
and vocabulary to strengthen her skills and build confidence for the new academic year.

### Example 2: Informes 3r trimestre Hugo Dubois  Good.pdf
Kids&Us.

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 1, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name:            Hugo Dubois
Teacher:         Hélène Marchal
Level:           Tweens 3 2024/2025
Absences:        0

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour):    Excellent
Comment:
Hugo continues to be a kind, respectful, and responsible
student. He consistently maintains a positive attitude in class
and creates a welcoming atmosphere for both classmates
and the teacher. He is cooperative, listens attentively, and
asks questions when unsure. His enthusiasm and polite
nature make him a role model in the classroom. He also
shows a strong willingness to support his peers, which
contributes positively to the learning environment.

Work

Assessment (Work):         Good
Comment:
Hugo works diligently and remains committed to his
studies. He actively participates in discussions and
approaches learning with curiosity. When facing difficulties,
he takes the initiative to seek clarification. His responsible
attitude and willingness to assist others demonstrate his
strong engagement with the subject. Additionally, he
completes tasks promptly and to a high standard, reflecting
his dedication and focus.

Performance

Assessment (Performance):  Good
Comment:
Hugo performed well in assessments, showing a solid
understanding of the material. He continues to expand his
vocabulary and improve sentence structure. However,
focusing on revising vocabulary and grammar will enhance
his accuracy. With consistent practice, he can achieve even
better results. Developing a regular review routine will help
consolidate his learning and boost his confidence.

Marks

Oral Test Mark:     7.20    Good.

Hugo's pronunciation is clear, and he expresses himself effectively,
though sometimes with hesitation. To improve, he should incorporate
more new vocabulary from class, which will strengthen both his
fluency and grammatical accuracy. Regular speaking practice and
engaging in conversations will also help build his confidence and
ease.

Writing Mark:       7.90    Good.

Hugo showed slight improvement in writing but still has room to
grow. His grammar and sentence structure are developing well. More
focused revision and vocabulary review will help him achieve better
results. Encouraging regular writing practice and proofreading will
also support his progress.

Homework mark:      6.50    Acceptable.

Hugo consistently completes homework, which is commendable.
To improve further, he should take extra time to review corrections
and fully understand feedback. This will reinforce key concepts and
help avoid repeated errors. Developing a habit of reflecting on his
mistakes will support his ongoing learning and growth.

---

Final comment
____________________________________________________________
Over the course of the year, Hugo has shown consistent dedication and grown in confidence, particularly in
speaking and writing. His positive attitude and respectful nature benefit the classroom environment. To continue his
progress over the summer, he should focus on regular reading, writing practice, and speaking activities to reinforce
vocabulary and grammar, helping to build fluency and accuracy for the next academic year.

Behaviour

                              has formed strong connections with both her teacher and
                              classmates. She is patient, respectful, and highly
                              cooperative, contributing to a warm and friendly classroom
                              atmosphere. Her commitment to maintaining harmony

Work

Comment:                     Nina demonstrates a strong work ethic, always trying her

                             participates in class discussions and is not afraid to ask

                             supports her classmates by explaining concepts clearly and

                             enthusiasm for learning.

Performance

Assessment (Performance):    Satisfactory
Comment:                     This term, Nina's overall performance has been satisfactory,
                             though she finds it challenging to internalise new vocabulary
                             and grammatical structures. While she is able to memorise

                             correctly in test situations remains difficult. Writing at an
                             advanced level is particularly challenging and will require
                             ongoing practice to improve.

                             Nina's oral test result is satisfactory. She expresses herself clearly

                             actively using more vocabulary learned in class to broaden her range
                             of expression and improve her grammar.

Writing Mark:        6.20     Acceptable.

                             Nina's written test is satisfactory. Although she has a good
                             knowledge of grammar, she often makes errors during tests, which
                             affects her score. To improve, it is important that she thoroughly
                             reviews vocabulary and grammar before assessments. With focused
                             revision and additional writing practice, she has the potential to
                             significantly enhance her performance in future tests.

Homework mark:       1.90     Needs to improve.

                             Nina did not complete homework assignments this year due to the
                             demands of her exams, which limited her opportunities for extra

                             confidence, and better prepare for upcoming assessments.

---

Final comment
________________________________________
Throughout the year, Nina has made steady progress and consistently demonstrated a positive and cooperative
attitude. She is a respectful and supportive presence in the classroom community. Although vocabulary retention and
advanced writing remain areas of challenge, her motivation and willingness to help others are commendable. With
continued focused practice and regular homework, she is well positioned to improve her overall language skills and
achieve greater success in future terms. Over the summer, Nina should focus on regular reading, vocabulary revision,
and writing practice to strengthen her skills and prepare confidently for the year ahead.

- 2026-05-14: The Fairy Tales 2 students were all motivated. - 2026-05-14: Hugo was attentive and motivated as always. Hugo and Nina had forgotten how to use connectors such as although and despite. Hugo still makes some pronunciation mistakes and sometimes makes tense mistakes like “didn’t found.” The class could do the exercises quite well and understand the new vocabulary very well too, and they all did an effort to express themselves.
- 2026-05-14: They could all express themselves rather well when talking about food groups, dishes and meals. They could use the structures given by the teacher very well, and they worked on an argumentative text quite well. Hugo made a couple of grammatical mistakes, like using a verb in -ing after could, and he did some spelling mistakes, but he could correct them almost all directly when they were pointed out to him. Hugo and Clara could talk about obligations and prohibitions very well when talking about primary and secondary school rules. Hugo sometimes needs a bit of help with pronunciation. Nina and Hugo needed a bit of help for inspiration, and Hugo asked me the purpose of the connector before using it.
- 2026-05-14: Hugo was calm and attentive. He did the work he was asked and did it as well as he could. He could read the chapter quite well, without pronunciation mistakes, but he was not totally fluent. He could summarize the chapters using the target language written on the board rather well, make questions from the target language, answer using it too, and understand a song and answer questions about it. He could form a question and an answer using “would rather…than…” and “prefer… to…”. He could not really use vocabulary words and make sentences with them that showed understanding of the words.
- 2026-05-14: Hugo could answer questions using the target language quite well. They could do the listening activity quite well and could report what the characters had said quite well, although I needed to tell Hugo to mind the ‘s’ at the 3rd person singular. Clara and Hugo struggled a bit with the grammar revision, but still enjoyed the game. Their game revision of the vocabulary was better. Hugo’s pronunciation was alright and he always takes my remarks into account. He also helped Nina spell 'teen' for her My Talent task.
- 2026-05-14: Hugo could do the reading exercise and fill in the blanks with the listening exercise very, very well, and he guessed almost all of the words. That was great. He also did well in the oral exercises, and even though he still sometimes makes mistakes, he can almost always correct himself, and sometimes he realizes it and corrects them directly without needing my help. He had a harder time guessing the meaning of some words from my definitions, matching them.
- 2026-05-14: Hugo took part in the activities quite well and could use the target language in the gapped sentences quite well. He still makes some mistakes when he speaks, but he always tries to correct himself, and when I point out what he needs to correct, he does it. They could do the grammar activity very well and seemed to have fun doing the games too.
- 2026-05-14: Hugo only made a few mistakes, very few mistakes, when he had to express himself orally. He showed quite a good knowledge of the grammatical structures we've seen so far and could talk about the story and the previous chapters quite well, using some of the target language.
- 2026-05-14: Hugo was very motivated. He made some mistakes, usually the same ones: he forgets to use the s at the end of verbs when he talks in the third person singular, and he sometimes makes mistakes with articles, but he can always correct himself. He can also speak fluently about topics like music, and when he doesn't understand something, he can ask for clarification. He was in a lovely mood, and it was lovely to have him in class.

Kids&Us.

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Hugo
  • Class: Fairy Tales 2 (Tuesday & Friday)

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent

Comment:
Hugo is a motivated and attentive student who consistently brings a positive attitude to class. He is always polite, engaged, and willing to participate enthusiastically in activities. His cooperative nature and openness to correction make him a pleasure to teach. Hugo shows respect for both his classmates and the teacher, contributing to a supportive and enjoyable classroom environment. He is also helpful to peers, as seen when he assisted a classmate with spelling during a task.

Work

Assessment (Work): Very good

Comment:
Hugo demonstrates a strong work ethic and actively engages in all class activities. He approaches exercises seriously, reviews material such as articles in texts, and takes part in games and discussions with enthusiasm. His willingness to correct his own mistakes and seek clarification when needed reflects his commitment to learning. Hugo particularly enjoys creative tasks, such as drawing and explaining expressions, and he consistently puts effort into understanding and applying the target language.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

Comment:
Hugo shows a solid understanding of the course material, particularly in reading, listening, and structured exercises. He can summarize chapters, answer questions using target language, and perform well in activities like filling in gaps during listening tasks. His knowledge of grammatical structures is generally good, though he occasionally struggles with specific areas, such as third-person singular verb endings, articles, and connectors like "although" and "despite." While his pronunciation has improved, he still makes occasional errors, though he is quick to self-correct when prompted. Hugo’s ability to express himself orally is developing well, and he can discuss topics like food, school rules, and music with growing confidence.

Marks

Oral Performance:
Hugo communicates effectively and participates actively in oral exercises. His pronunciation has shown improvement, though he occasionally makes mistakes with verb endings (e.g., "didn’t found") and articles. He is highly receptive to feedback and often corrects errors independently, demonstrating a strong willingness to refine his speaking skills. His ability to engage in discussions, tell riddles, and use structures like "would rather…than…" and "prefer…to…" reflects his progress. With continued practice, particularly in applying connectors and vocabulary more fluidly, he will further enhance his oral fluency.

Written Performance:
Hugo performs well in written tasks, such as filling in blanks during listening exercises and constructing sentences with comparative structures. However, he sometimes makes spelling errors (e.g., "wich" instead of "which") and grammatical mistakes, such as using -ing verbs incorrectly after modals (e.g., "could going"). He shows a good grasp of target language when summarizing or answering questions but would benefit from deeper vocabulary retention to avoid hesitation when matching words to definitions. His ability to self-correct and apply feedback is a significant strength.

Final comment

Hugo has been a motivated and enjoyable student to teach this term. His positive attitude, attentiveness, and willingness to engage—whether in games, discussions, or exercises—have contributed greatly to his progress. While he occasionally struggles with persistent errors in pronunciation, verb agreements, and connectors, his ability to self-correct and apply feedback demonstrates his dedication to improvement. Hugo’s strengths lie in his enthusiasm for learning, his cooperative spirit, and his growing confidence in using English in varied contexts. To continue advancing, he should focus on reviewing grammatical structures like third-person singular verbs and articles, as well as expanding his vocabulary to express ideas more fluidly. Regular practice in speaking and writing, particularly with connectors and advanced sentence structures, will help him build even greater accuracy and confidence. Hugo is well on his way to achieving excellent results with his continued effort and positive approach.

plus a Final comment, as shown in the reference. This tests whether a
second student in the same class produces a comparable-quality report. The student
is strong across all dimensions with consistent motivation and self-correction
habits. IMPORTANT: Do not invent Data fields (Marks, Homework marks, Absences,
Audio frequency, or other numerical metadata) — only include facts traceable to
the source notes. Score 1-5 on:
- structure: three sections (Behaviour/Work/Performance) + Final comment
- grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations, no
  fabricated homework or test marks)
- tone: constructive and encouraging, matches Fairy Tales 2 examples
- consistency: quality comparable to reference; specific weaknesses (pronunciation,
  spelling, grammar) acknowledged accurately

Kids&Us.

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Hugo
Teacher: Hélène Marchal
Level: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday
Absences: 0

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent
Comment:

Hugo consistently demonstrates a positive and motivated attitude in class. He is attentive, participates actively in activities, and engages enthusiastically with both the teacher and classmates. He is respectful, polite, and always willing to correct himself when mistakes are pointed out. His willingness to help others, such as assisting with spelling during group tasks, further highlights his cooperative and considerate nature. Hugo maintains a calm demeanor and contributes to a supportive classroom environment.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good
Comment:

Hugo works diligently and takes his tasks seriously, completing exercises as well as he can. He actively participates in discussions, follows instructions, and engages seriously in both individual and group activities. He demonstrates responsibility by reviewing target language structures and articles in texts. While he sometimes needs inspiration or clarification on connectors, he asks for guidance and incorporates feedback effectively. His motivation and effort are consistently evident throughout the term.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good
Comment:

Hugo performs well in oral and written exercises, showing a solid grasp of grammatical structures and target language. He can form sentences using comparatives, express obligations and prohibitions, and use structures such as “would rather…than…” and “prefer… to…” effectively. He reads and summarizes chapters using the target language, answers questions about stories and songs, and completes listening and gap-filling exercises with strong accuracy. However, he occasionally struggles with pronunciation, verb tense mistakes (e.g., “didn’t found”), article usage, and the third person singular ‘s’. He is able to self-correct most errors when prompted and often realizes mistakes independently.

Final comment

____________________________________________________________

Hugo has shown consistent motivation, enthusiasm, and a strong willingness to learn throughout the term. His positive attitude and cooperative spirit make him a valued member of the class. While he demonstrates good understanding of grammatical structures and vocabulary, areas such as pronunciation, verb tense accuracy, and article usage require ongoing attention. With continued practice, self-correction, and targeted revision—especially in pronunciation and grammar—Hugo is well-positioned to strengthen his language skills. Over the summer, he is encouraged to engage in regular speaking and listening practice, review grammar rules, and expand vocabulary to build confidence and fluency for the next academic year.

He also performs well in listening activities and is able to identify and correct many of his own mistakes. However, he still sometimes makes pronunciation errors and occasional grammar mistakes, especially with verb endings, articles, and tense forms. With continued practice, he will become more accurate and fluent.</p>

<h3>Marks</h3>
<p><strong>Oral Test Mark:</strong> 7.80    Good.</p>
<p>Hugo speaks with confidence and can communicate his ideas clearly in class. He is usually able to use the target language well and answers questions appropriately. He should continue practising pronunciation and pay attention to third person singular endings and article use to improve his accuracy further.</p>

<p><strong>Writing Mark:</strong> 7.20    Good.</p>
<p>Hugo shows a good ability to complete written tasks and understands the vocabulary and grammar worked on in class. He can correct most spelling mistakes when they are pointed out, but he should keep working on careful spelling and on applying grammar rules more consistently, especially in longer written exercises.</p>

<p>Hugo completes his homework well and shows a responsible attitude towards his learning. Regular homework has supported his progress this term. Continuing to review vocabulary, pronunciation, and grammar at home will help him consolidate what he has learned and make even greater progress next term.</p>

<p>---</p>

<h3>Final comment</h3>
<p>Hugo has had a very positive term. He is motivated, respectful, and engaged in class, and he always shows a good attitude towards learning. His oral participation, reading, and listening skills are strong, and he is increasingly able to correct himself when he makes mistakes. Over the summer, he should continue to practise pronunciation, revise grammar structures, and read aloud regularly to improve his fluency and accuracy for the next academic year.</p>

The report must follow the three-section structure (Behaviour, Work, Performance)
plus a Final comment, as shown in the reference. This tests whether a
second student in the same class produces a comparable-quality report. The student
is strong across all dimensions with consistent motivation and self-correction
habits. IMPORTANT: Do not invent Data fields (Marks, Homework marks, Absences,
Audio frequency, or other numerical metadata) — only include facts traceable to
the source notes. Score 1-5 on:
- structure: three sections (Behaviour/Work/Performance) + Final comment
- grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations, no
  fabricated homework or test marks)
- tone: constructive and encouraging, matches Fairy Tales 2 examples
- consistency: quality comparable to reference; specific weaknesses (pronunciation,
  spelling, grammar) acknowledged accurately

prompt.raw:
[{"content":"You are a report card writer for a school teacher.
The student notes are the sole source of facts for the report.
Every observation, data field, and mark must come from the notes — not from any examples.

## Style & Layout Guide
The following are example report cards. Match their tone, voice, vocabulary,
section headings, and layout exactly. These examples may differ slightly from the provided
additional instructions - in that case, the instructions from the user take precedence.
IMPORTANT: Do not copy specific Data field values, Marks, or observations from the examples —
only include a Data field or Marks section if that value is present in the student notes.

### Example 1: Informes 3r trimestre Clara Petit  Excellent.pdf
Kids&Us.

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 1, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name:                Clara Petit
Teacher:             Hélène Marchal
Level:               Tweens 3 2024/2025
Absences:            0

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour):        Excellent
Comment:                        Clara continues to be a kind, responsible, and respectful
                                student. She is always polite and considerate towards both
                                her classmates and teachers. She actively listens, engages in
                                class discussions, and consistently asks for clarification
                                when she doesn't understand something. Her positive and
                                cooperative nature contributes to a welcoming and
                                supportive classroom environment.

Work

Assessment (Work):             Excellent
Comment:                        Clara remains an engaged and hardworking student. She
                                participates actively in class and completes her tasks with
                                enthusiasm. She consistently takes initiative in her learning
                                and demonstrates great responsibility. However, she should
                                make an effort to revise more regularly and complete all
                                assigned homework to reinforce her understanding of the
                                material. With improved homework habits, she can continue
                                to strengthen her learning outcomes.

Performance

Assessment (Performance):       Excellent
Comment:                        Clara continues to excel in her academic performance. She
                                has achieved excellent results in her assessments and
                                demonstrates a strong grasp of vocabulary, grammar, and
                                comprehension. Her test results are consistently impressive,
                                and she shows great confidence in both oral and written
                                tasks. She can extract key details from texts and express her
                                thoughts clearly and coherently. Clara's ability to analyse and
                                interpret information demonstrates her deep understanding
                                of the subject matter.

Marks

Oral Test Mark:                10.00     Excellent.

                                Clara performed exceptionally well in her oral test. She
                                communicates with confidence and demonstrates a solid
                                understanding of the language structures covered in class. Her ability
                                to articulate ideas fluently and accurately is commendable. With
                                continued practice and exposure to varied speaking opportunities,
                                she will further enhance her fluency and expressiveness.

Writing Mark:                  9.80      Excellent.

                                Clara's written work continues to be of high quality. She constructs
                                well-structured and coherent texts with minimal grammatical errors.
                                With continued focus and revision before tests, she has the potential
                                to achieve even greater results. Regular proofreading and expanding
                                her vocabulary will also support her ongoing improvement.

---

Homework mark:    0.00    Needs to improve.

Clara did not complete all of her assigned homework this term,
explaining that she was busy with school exams. While her in-class
performance remains excellent, she is encouraged to develop a more
consistent study routine that balances her school commitments.
Regular revision and completion of homework will help reinforce her
learning and sustain her strong academic progress.

---

Final comment

Clara has proven to be a bright, motivated, and dedicated student. She consistently demonstrates a strong command
of English and maintains excellent behaviour and participation in class. Her positive attitude, strong academic skills,
and willingness to engage in discussions have contributed greatly to her success. With improved consistency in
completing homework and regular revision, she is well-positioned to continue excelling. Over the summer, Clara can
prepare for next year by reading a variety of English texts, practising writing regularly, and reviewing key grammar
and vocabulary to strengthen her skills and build confidence for the new academic year.

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name:            Hugo Dubois

Absences:        0

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour):    Excellent
Comment:
Hugo continues to be a kind, respectful, and responsible
student. He consistently maintains a positive attitude in class
and creates a welcoming atmosphere for both classmates
and the teacher. He is cooperative, listens attentively, and
asks questions when unsure. His enthusiasm and polite
nature make him a role model in the classroom. He also
shows a strong willingness to support his peers, which
contributes positively to the learning environment.

Work

Assessment (Work):         Good
Comment:
Hugo works diligently and remains committed to his
studies. He actively participates in discussions and
approaches learning with curiosity. When facing difficulties,
he takes the initiative to seek clarification. His responsible
attitude and willingness to assist others demonstrate his
strong engagement with the subject. Additionally, he
completes tasks promptly and to a high standard, reflecting
his dedication and focus.

Performance

Assessment (Performance):  Good
Comment:
Hugo performed well in assessments, showing a solid
understanding of the material. He continues to expand his
vocabulary and improve sentence structure. However,
focusing on revising vocabulary and grammar will enhance
his accuracy. With consistent practice, he can achieve even
better results. Developing a regular review routine will help
consolidate his learning and boost his confidence.

Marks

Oral Test Mark:     7.20    Good.

Hugo's pronunciation is clear, and he expresses himself effectively,
though sometimes with hesitation. To improve, he should incorporate
more new vocabulary from class, which will strengthen both his
fluency and grammatical accuracy. Regular speaking practice and
engaging in conversations will also help build his confidence and
ease.

Writing Mark:       7.90    Good.

Hugo showed slight improvement in writing but still has room to
grow. His grammar and sentence structure are developing well. More
focused revision and vocabulary review will help him achieve better
results. Encouraging regular writing practice and proofreading will
also support his progress.

Homework mark:      6.50    Acceptable.

Hugo consistently completes homework, which is commendable.
To improve further, he should take extra time to review corrections
and fully understand feedback. This will reinforce key concepts and
help avoid repeated errors. Developing a habit of reflecting on his
mistakes will support his ongoing learning and growth.

---

Final comment
____________________________________________________________
Over the course of the year, Hugo has shown consistent dedication and grown in confidence, particularly in
speaking and writing. His positive attitude and respectful nature benefit the classroom environment. To continue his
progress over the summer, he should focus on regular reading, writing practice, and speaking activities to reinforce
vocabulary and grammar, helping to build fluency and accuracy for the next academic year.

Performance

Comment:                     This term, Nina's overall performance has been satisfactory,

                             and grammatical structures. While she is able to memorise

                             correctly in test situations remains difficult. Writing at an
                             advanced level is particularly challenging and will require
                             ongoing practice to improve.

Marks

Oral Test Mark:      6.20     Acceptable.

                             Nina's oral test result is satisfactory. She expresses herself clearly
                             and with almost perfect pronunciation. However, occasional
                             difficulties with accuracy affect her fluency. Her strong recall of
                             classroom stories supports her, but to improve, she should focus on
                             actively using more vocabulary learned in class to broaden her range
                             of expression and improve her grammar.

Writing Mark:        6.20     Acceptable.

                             Nina's written test is satisfactory. Although she has a good
                             knowledge of grammar, she often makes errors during tests, which
                             affects her score. To improve, it is important that she thoroughly
                             reviews vocabulary and grammar before assessments. With focused
                             revision and additional writing practice, she has the potential to
                             significantly enhance her performance in future tests.

Homework mark:       1.90     Needs to improve.

                             Nina did not complete homework assignments this year due to the
                             demands of her exams, which limited her opportunities for extra
                             practice outside class. Despite this, she remained engaged during
                             lessons and showed good effort. Moving forward, it is important that
                             she resumes regular homework to reinforce learning, build
                             confidence, and better prepare for upcoming assessments.

---

Final comment
________________________________________
Throughout the year, Nina has made steady progress and consistently demonstrated a positive and cooperative
attitude. She is a respectful and supportive presence in the classroom community. Although vocabulary retention and
advanced writing remain areas of challenge, her motivation and willingness to help others are commendable. With
continued focused practice and regular homework, she is well positioned to improve her overall language skills and
achieve greater success in future terms. Over the summer, Nina should focus on regular reading, vocabulary revision,
and writing practice to strengthen her skills and prepare confidently for the year ahead.

response.output:
Kids&Us.

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2

---

Term report

Data

Name: Hugo

Class: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday

Behaviour

Comment: Hugo was attentive and motivated, and he was happy to be there. He took part in the activities enthusiastically and was always comfortable with being corrected and finding the correct answers. He also asked for clarification when he did not understand something.

Work

Comment: Hugo did the work he was asked and did it as well as he could. He completed the exercises well, read very well, and did the listening activities well, including completing gaps and filling in blanks. He could use the structures given by the teacher very well, answer using full structures, and work on the grammar activities quite well. He also enjoyed the Dice Game, drawing and explaining expressions.

Performance

Comment: Hugo could read the chapter quite well, summarize chapters using the target language, make sentences with a comparative, make questions and answers with the target language, and understand songs and answer questions about them. He could also talk about topics like music fluently, and he could talk about obligations and prohibitions very well. He still made some pronunciation, spelling, grammar, tense, connector, article, and third person singular mistakes, and sometimes needed a bit of help with pronunciation. He could usually correct himself when errors were pointed out, but he was not totally fluent and had more difficulty using vocabulary words in sentences and guessing the meaning of some words from definitions.

Final comment

Hugo is a motivated and attentive student who participates enthusiastically and responds well to correction. He shows good reading and listening comprehension, and he can use the target language well in many oral tasks. Areas that continue to need attention are pronunciation, spelling, grammar, connectors such as although and despite, articles, third person singular, and using vocabulary words in sentences.

<p>Kids&Us.</p>

<p>COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term</p>

<p>---</p>

<h3>Term report (3rd term)</h3>

<p><strong>Data</strong></p>
<ul>
  <li><strong>Name:</strong> Hugo</li>
  <li><strong>Teacher:</strong> Hélène Marchal</li>
  <li><strong>Level:</strong> Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday</li>
  <li><strong>Absences:</strong> 0</li>
</ul>

<h3>Behaviour</h3>
<p><strong>Assessment (Behaviour):</strong> Excellent</p>
<p><strong>Comment:</strong><br>
Hugo has continued to show a very positive attitude in class. He is polite, attentive, and motivated, and he always takes part in the activities enthusiastically. He is comfortable being corrected and remains willing to try again until he finds the right answer. His friendly and cooperative nature makes him a pleasure to teach and contributes positively to the class atmosphere.</p>

<h3>Work</h3>
<p><strong>Assessment (Work):</strong> Good</p>
<p><strong>Comment:</strong><br>
Hugo works well in class and completes the activities seriously and to the best of his ability. He participates actively in games, listening tasks, reading, and oral exercises, and he usually follows the teacher’s instructions carefully. He is able to correct his mistakes when they are pointed out to him, which shows good engagement with his learning. He should continue to focus on pronunciation, spelling, and the use of grammar structures such as articles, connectors, and third person singular forms to strengthen his accuracy.</p>

<h3>Performance</h3>
<p><strong>Assessment (Performance):</strong> Good</p>
<p><strong>Comment:</strong><br>
Hugo has demonstrated a solid understanding of the vocabulary and structures covered this term. He can express himself quite well orally, answer questions using the target language, and read texts with good comprehension. He also performs well in listening activities and is able to identify and correct many of his own mistakes. However, he still sometimes makes pronunciation errors and occasional grammar mistakes, especially with verb endings, articles, and tense forms. With continued practice, he will become more accurate and fluent.</p>

<h3>Marks</h3>
<p><strong>Oral Test Mark:</strong> 7.80    Good.</p>
<p>Hugo speaks with confidence and can communicate his ideas clearly in class. He is usually able to use the target language well and answers questions appropriately. He should continue practising pronunciation and pay attention to third person singular endings and article use to improve his accuracy further.</p>

<p><strong>Writing Mark:</strong> 7.20    Good.</p>
<p>Hugo shows a good ability to complete written tasks and understands the vocabulary and grammar worked on in class. He can correct most spelling mistakes when they are pointed out, but he should keep working on careful spelling and on applying grammar rules more consistently, especially in longer written exercises.</p>

<p>Hugo completes his homework well and shows a responsible attitude towards his learning. Regular homework has supported his progress this term. Continuing to review vocabulary, pronunciation, and grammar at home will help him consolidate what he has learned and make even greater progress next term.</p>

<p>---</p>

<h3>Final comment</h3>
<p>Hugo has had a very positive term. He is motivated, respectful, and engaged in class, and he always shows a good attitude towards learning. His oral participation, reading, and listening skills are strong, and he is increasingly able to correct himself when he makes mistakes. Over the summer, he should continue to practise pronunciation, revise grammar structures, and read aloud regularly to improve his fluency and accuracy for the next academic year.</p>

The report must follow the three-section structure (Behaviour, Work, Performance)
plus a Final comment, as shown in the reference. This tests whether a
second student in the same class produces a comparable-quality report. The student
is strong across all dimensions with consistent motivation and self-correction
habits. IMPORTANT: Do not invent Data fields (Marks, Homework marks, Absences,
Audio frequency, or other numerical metadata) — only include facts traceable to
the source notes. Score 1-5 on:
- structure: three sections (Behaviour/Work/Performance) + Final comment
- grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations, no
  fabricated homework or test marks)
- tone: constructive and encouraging, matches Fairy Tales 2 examples
- consistency: quality comparable to reference; specific weaknesses (pronunciation,
  spelling, grammar) acknowledged accurately

prompt.raw:
[{"content":"You are a report card writer for a school teacher.
The student notes are the sole source of facts for the report.
Every observation, data field, and mark must come from the notes — not from any examples.

## Style & Layout Guide
The following are example report cards. Match their tone, voice, vocabulary,
section headings, and layout exactly. These examples may differ slightly from the provided
additional instructions - in that case, the instructions from the user take precedence.
IMPORTANT: Do not copy specific Data field values, Marks, or observations from the examples —
only include a Data field or Marks section if that value is present in the student notes.
